[[JA:加佐美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398477}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=加佐美神社 | 画像=[[File:Kasami jinja buden.JPG|270px|拝殿]]
加佐美神社拝殿（2011年2月） | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|各務原市|Q641491}}{{jalink|蘇原古市場町|Q131870109}}五丁目1番地 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=加佐美大神
{{jalink|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|Q3488899}}
{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}} | 創建={{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}6年（{{jalink|864年|Q25041}}） | 本殿=三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月14日|Q2922}}・{{jalink|10月15日|Q2919|15日}} | 神事={{jalink|#主な神事}}を参照 | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=加佐美神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''加佐美神社'''（かさみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|各務原市|Q641491}}{{jalink|蘇原古市場町|Q131870109}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|新境川|Q11501642}}の左岸、加佐美山の東南東に位置する。 {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}である。元々は蘇原10カ村（{{jalink|伊飛島村|Q18990514|伊吹・飛鳥・島崎}}・{{jalink|和合村 (岐阜県稲葉郡)|Q23940976|野口・熊田・東島・坂井}}・{{jalink|大宮村 (岐阜県)|Q18748127|大島・宮代}}・{{jalink|古市場村 (岐阜県各務郡)|Q22125133|古市場}}）の{{jalink|総社|Q1107129}}であるが、現在は{{jalink|蘇原町|Q11625179|蘇原地区}}の総社、{{jalink|産土神|Q1392097}}とされている。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は郷社。 各務原市内の{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}五社のうちの一社である[https://www.tezikarao.org/news/detail.php?id=149 各務原市金幣五社巡り]、岐阜県神社庁各務原市支部。 === 概略 === {{jalink|864年|Q25041}}（{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}6年）、{{jalink|清和天皇|Q349243}}により創建。かつてこの地を{{jalink|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|Q3488899}}などの{{jalink|蘇我氏|Q909854}}が治めており、「蘇原」の地名は、蘇我氏の領有に由来するといわれている。蘇我倉山田石川麻呂が亡くなった後、曽原荘（現在の蘇原）の人々が蘇我倉山田石川麻呂を崇敬し、祭祀したのが始まりといわれており、神社自体は{{jalink|7世紀|Q8089}}には存在した可能性がある。{{jalink|905年|Q24239}}（{{jalink|延喜|Q1374325}}5年）ころ、{{jalink|各務郡|Q11412369}}曽原荘古市場にあり八幡宮と呼ばれていたという。{{jalink|908年|Q30463}}（延喜8年）には笠見明神と称されたという。なお、{{jalink|1912年|Q2077}}（明治45年）発行の『{{jalink|府県郷社明治神社誌料|Q11486103|府縣郷社明治神社誌料}}』中巻によれば、{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}及び創建年は不詳としている{{Cite journal |和書 |title=岐阜縣之部 |journal={{jalink|府県郷社明治神社誌料|Q11486103|府縣郷社明治神社誌料}} |volume=中巻 |publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所 |date=1912-01-15 |pages=36-37 |doi=10.11501/1088278}}。 『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156|美濃國}}各務郡七座のうちに「加佐美神社」とあり{{Cite journal |和書 |title=延喜式 卷十 神祇十 神名下 |journal={{jalink|国史大系|Q10926780|國史大系}} |volume=13 |publisher={{jalink|経済雑誌社|Q11606486}} |date=1900-11-15 |pages=346 |doi=10.11501/991103}}、『美濃國神名帳』では各務郡座二十三社のうちに「{{jalink|正四位|Q11123338|正四位下}}笠見田明神」と記されている{{Cite journal |和書 |editor={{jalink|佐伯有義|Q11382847}} 等 |title=延暦寺本美濃国神名帳 |journal=神祇全書 |volume=第4輯 |publisher={{jalink|皇典講究所|Q11580997}} |date=1908-05-17 |pages=624 |doi=10.11501/991328}}{{Cite journal |和書 |editor=各務原市教育委員会 |title=第二節 延喜式神名帳にみる神社 |journal=各務原市史 |volume=通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=各務原市 |date=1986-12-20 |pages=626-630 |doi=10.11501/9571788}}。なお、『各務原市史』では「『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』貞観6年（864年）8月15日{{jalink|己巳|Q1062039}}条には「{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|従五位|Q11071125|従五位下}}加佐美神授ク二従五位上ヲ一」とみえる」と記すが{{Efn|{{jalink|引用|Q206287}}に際して{{jalink|漢数字|Q683566}}を{{jalink|アラビア数字|Q29961325}}に変更。}}、写本によっては記載が異なる{{Efn|{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}年間{{jalink|松下見林|Q92923373}}校正本を底本とする{{jalink|1914年|Q2083}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}3年）発行の『日本三代実録』では「美濃国」とするが{{Cite journal |和書 |title=三代実録卷九 清和天皇（貞觀六年八月） |journal=日本三代實録 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |date=1914-02-28 |pages=184 |quote=十五日己巳。美濃國従五位下長田神。免上神田神。加佐美神。（中略）並授二従五位上一 |doi=10.11501/950691}}、{{jalink|宮内省|Q8193428}}{{jalink|図書寮|Q11420348}}所蔵谷森健男氏旧蔵本を底本とする{{jalink|1944年|Q5268}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}19年）発行の『日本三代実録』では「{{jalink|美作国|Q936036}}」とする{{Cite journal |和書 |title=三代実録卷九 清和天皇（貞觀六年八月） |journal=日本三代實録 |publisher=大八洲出版創立事務所 |date=1944-06-28 |pages=140 |quote=十五日己巳。美作國従五位下長田神。免上神田神。前社神。（中略）並授二従五位上一 |doi=10.11501/3456452}}。}}。 境内には{{jalink|阿弥陀堂|Q11657414}}、{{jalink|薬師堂|Q11622438}}があり、{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}の名残がある。かつては{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}として根本寺が存在したが、明治初期に{{jalink|廃仏毀釈|Q702298}}で{{jalink|廃寺|Q11486287}}となっている。本殿（{{jalink|1687年|Q7668}}建築）、幣殿（{{jalink|1910年|Q2075}}建築）、拝殿（{{jalink|1746年|Q7589}}建築）は、{{jalink|2008|Q1069941}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}20年）4月18日に国の{{jalink|登録有形文化財|Q11579194}}に登録されている{{Cite web |和書 |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami3.html |title=加佐美神社本殿 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami3.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{文化遺産オンライン|144291|加佐美神社本殿}}{{Cite web |和書 |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami2.html |title=加佐美神社幣殿 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami2.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{文化遺産オンライン|172332|加佐美神社幣殿}}{{Cite web |和書 |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami1.html |title=加佐美神社拝殿 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami1.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{文化遺産オンライン|186933|加佐美神社拝殿}}『式内金幣社加佐美神社の栞』。 蘇我氏とつながりが深く、周辺には蘇我石川麻呂の墳墓（{{jalink|伝蘇我倉山田石川麻呂の墓|Q11381424}}）や、山田寺跡（{{jalink|山田寺塔心礎|Q11470319}}）がある。 {{jalink|1975年|Q2479}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}50年）9月16日、岐阜県神社長指定第76号により金幣社に指定される{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=780 |title=加佐美神社 |publisher=岐阜県神社庁 |accessdate=2025-05-09}}。 {{jalink|参道|Q667783}}は{{jalink|各務原市#市内の道路通称名|Q641491|蘇原中央通り}}の一部に該当し、{{jalink|御旅所|Q11489117}}は参道沿いの蘇原古市場町二丁目にある。かつては参道には二ノ鳥居と三ノ鳥居があり、二ノ鳥居は蘇原中央通りと{{jalink|各務原市#市内の道路通称名|Q641491|おがせ街道}}との交点、三ノ鳥居は蘇原中央通りと{{jalink|各務原市#市内の道路通称名|Q641491|山田寺通り}}との交点に存在したといわれている{{Cite book |和書 |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/008/305/03_kasami240815.pdf |title=各務原市 地区別景観計画 風景形成基準ガイドライン 重点風景地区 3 加佐美神社地区 |format=PDF |publisher=各務原市 |page=1 |accessdate=2025-02-26}}。 かつて加佐美神社には一之宮、二之宮、三之宮があり、一之宮は各務郡古市場村（現・蘇原古市場町）の{{jalink|神明神社 (各務原市蘇原古市場町)|Q134987025|神明神社}}、二之宮は各務郡伊吹村（現・{{jalink|蘇原伊吹町|Q131900799}}）の{{jalink|八坂神社 (各務原市蘇原伊吹町)|Q134928112|八坂神社}}、三之宮は各務郡野口村（現・{{jalink|蘇原野口町|Q131870111}}）の熊野神社{{Efn|{{jalink|1909年|Q2057}}（明治42年）、{{jalink|神明神社 (各務原市蘇原野口町)|Q134926762|神明神社}}（{{jalink|蘇原野口町|Q131870111}}）に合祀。}}である現地解説板「加佐美神社「二の鳥居」の台石」より。各務原市{{jalink|景観条例|Q11514689|都市景観条例}}に基づき、{{jalink|2008年|Q2004}}（平成20年）8月1日に各務原市告示第80号で神社を含む周辺の約37.29{{jalink|ヘクタール|Q35852}}が重点風景地区に指定されている{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/shisei/shisaku/1008207/1008297/1008302/1008305.html |title=重点風景地区（決定） 歴史的資源・風致を有する地区 |publisher=各務原市 |date=2024-08-16 |accessdate=2025-05-09}}。 {{jalink|戦国時代 (日本)|Q204023|戦国時代}}には加佐美神社は{{jalink|八幡宮|Q11390939}}と呼ばれ、神社の裏山の加佐美山も八幡山と呼ばれており、{{jalink|八幡山城 (美濃国)|Q38276751|八幡山城}}が存在した。 === 祭神 === * 加佐美大神 ** 一説によると、かつては加佐美神社の西北西にある「加佐美山」自体が信仰の対象であったという。加佐美大神は加佐美山を神としたと推測される。 * {{jalink|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|Q3488899}} * {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} === 境内社 === * 秋葉神社（{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|迦遇槌神}}） * 御鍬神社（{{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|豊受大神}}） * 加佐美稲荷神社（{{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|倉稲魂命}}） * 神明神社（{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}） === その他 === * {{jalink|庚申塔|Q11486001}} ** かつての加佐美神社の二の鳥居の礎石の一つを使用し、{{jalink|1980年|Q2439}}（昭和55年）建立現地解説板「加佐美神社庚申塔について」より。 * {{jalink|忠魂碑|Q11274518}}（{{jalink|日清戦争|Q178687|征清紀念碑}}、{{jalink|日露戦争|Q159950|日露戦役}}忠魂碑、殉国戦士碑） ** 元は別の場所に設置されていたのを境内に移転。 === 主な神事 === 『式内金幣社加佐美神社の栞』による。 * 1月1日 - {{jalink|歳旦祭|Q11546824}}・開運祈禱祭・交通安全祭 * 2月節分 - {{jalink|節分|Q746798}}祭 * 旧暦2月14日 - 秋葉祭 * 2月20日 - {{jalink|祈年祭|Q118906710}}・御鍬祭 * 3月15日 - 住吉祭 * 4月第一土曜日 - 奥之宮祭・水神祭 * 7月最終日曜日 - {{jalink|大祓|Q11261910}}神事 * 10月15日以前の第二日曜日 - {{jalink|例祭|Q11385469|例大祭}} ** かつては毎年10月14日・15日に行われていた。花御輿が繰り出される。以前はけんか神輿が奉納されており、事故で一時中断していたが、{{jalink|蘇原六軒町|Q131900816}}を中心とする「六軒みこしの会」により、けんか神輿を模擬演舞として復活している{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://rokken-mikoshi.jimdosite.com/ |title=六軒みこしの会 |accessdate=2025-05-09}}{{Cite news |和書 |url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/791540 |title=垂直に立て「けんかみこし」 各務原加佐美神社で例大祭 |newspaper={{jalink|中日新聞|Q904678}} |date=2023-10-19}}。 * 11月15日 - {{jalink|七五三|Q1072387}}祭 * 11月23日 - 新穀感謝祭 * 12月31日 - {{jalink|除夜|Q7245702}}祭 * 毎月1日・15日 - 月次祭 === 文化財 === * 木造狛犬 ** 高さ約115cmの木製の{{jalink|狛犬|Q20155846}}。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}前期の製作と推測される。市指定文化財（{{jalink|1974年|Q2478}}（昭和49年）6月26日）{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=市指定文化財 |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=各務原市}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005217.html |title=実は「狛犬」は片方だけ？優美な木造の彫刻 |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=各務原市}}。 * 加佐美神社獅子頭 ** {{jalink|延宝|Q1145735}}3年（{{jalink|1675年|Q7624}}）各務郡野口村から寄進された木造の獅子頭。10月の例大祭の神幸行列では、獅子頭が神輿の行列を先導する。市指定文化財（{{jalink|1970年|Q2474}}（昭和45年）3月23日）{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005225.html |title=「おししさま」の伝承が伝わる地域の宝 |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=各務原市}}。 === 所在地 === * {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|各務原市|Q641491}}{{jalink|蘇原古市場町|Q131870109}}五丁目1番地 === 交通機関 === * {{jalink|岐阜乗合自動車|Q5560101|岐阜バス}}尾崎団地線「蘇原」{{jalink|バス停留所|Q953806|バス停}}下車、徒歩で約10分。 ** {{jalink|JR岐阜駅バスターミナル|Q11226229}}13番のりば「諏訪山団地」「{{jalink|岐阜県立各務原高等学校|Q11471490|各務原高校}}前」「{{jalink|テクノプラザ (岐阜県)|Q11319203|テクノプラザ}}」「{{jalink|三柿野駅|Q6845663}}」行きに乗車。 ** {{jalink|名鉄岐阜のりば|Q11415771}}6番のりば「諏訪山団地」「各務原高校前」「テクノプラザ」「三柿野駅」行に乗車。 * {{jalink|各務原市ふれあいバス|Q11412316}} ** 蘇原線「蘇原古市場町」バス停下車、徒歩で約8分。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|Q11496922}} * {{jalink|村国神社|Q11523055}} * {{jalink|村国真墨田神社|Q11523053}} * {{jalink|御井神社 (各務原市)|Q11488771}} * {{jalink|岐阜県の登録有形文化財一覧|Q119930208}} === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat|Kasami-jinja}} * {{Instagram|kasami_jinjya_|加佐美神社}} * {{kotobank}} * [https://kakamigahara-kankou.jp/tourism/256 加佐美神社｜かかみがはらさんぽ（各務原市観光情報）] * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_7128.html 加佐美神社｜観光スポット｜岐阜県観光公式サイト「岐阜の旅ガイド」] {{DEFAULTSORT:かさみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kasami == Japanese content == Kasami Shrine
Kasami Shrine is a Shinto shrine in Sohara-Furuichiba, Kakamigahara, Gifu Prefecture, Japan.
Status - Engishiki-listed shrine (minor category) - Former rank: village-area chief shrine (gosha) - One of the traditional "Kinpeisha Five Shrines" in Kakamigahara
Enshrined deities - Kasami Okami - Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro - Emperor Ojin
Historical outline - A traditional foundation date of 864 is transmitted. - Local tradition says the shrine developed from the veneration of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro by residents of the old Sohara estate. - The site may have had an older origin, possibly from the 7th century. - It appears in medieval and early-modern records as a local key shrine of the Sohara villages.
The precinct historically included Buddhist-related structures, showing traces of premodern shrine-temple syncretism.
[[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]]

== Japanese content == Kasami Shrine
Kasami Shrine is a Shinto shrine in Sohara-Furuichiba, Kakamigahara, Gifu Prefecture, Japan.
Status - Engishiki-listed shrine (minor category) - Former rank: village-area chief shrine (gosha) - One of the traditional "Kinpeisha Five Shrines" in Kakamigahara
Enshrined deities - Kasami Okami - Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro - Emperor Ojin
Historical outline - A traditional foundation date of 864 is transmitted. - Local tradition says the shrine developed from the veneration of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro by residents of the old Sohara estate. - The site may have had an older origin, possibly from the 7th century. - It appears in medieval and early-modern records as a local key shrine of the Sohara villages.
The precinct historically included Buddhist-related structures, showing traces of premodern shrine-temple syncretism.
[[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]]

== Japanese content == Kasami Shrine
Kasami Shrine is a Shinto shrine in Sohara-Furuichiba, Kakamigahara, Gifu Prefecture, Japan.
Status - Engishiki-listed shrine (minor category) - Former rank: village-area chief shrine (gosha) - One of the traditional "Kinpeisha Five Shrines" in Kakamigahara
Enshrined deities - Kasami Okami - Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro - Emperor Ojin
Historical outline - A traditional foundation date of 864 is transmitted. - Local tradition says the shrine developed from the veneration of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro by residents of the old Sohara estate. - The site may have had an older origin, possibly from the 7th century. - It appears in medieval and early-modern records as a local key shrine of the Sohara villages.
The precinct historically included Buddhist-related structures, showing traces of premodern shrine-temple syncretism.

== Japanese content == Kasami Shrine
Kasami Shrine is a Shinto shrine in Sohara-Furuichiba, Kakamigahara, Gifu Prefecture, Japan.
Status - Engishiki-listed shrine (minor category) - Former rank: village-area chief shrine (gosha) - One of the traditional "Kinpeisha Five Shrines" in Kakamigahara
Enshrined deities - Kasami Okami - Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro - Emperor Ojin
Historical outline - A traditional foundation date of 864 is transmitted. - Local tradition says the shrine developed from the veneration of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro by residents of the old Sohara estate. - The site may have had an older origin, possibly from the 7th century. - It appears in medieval and early-modern records as a local key shrine of the Sohara villages.
The precinct historically included Buddhist-related structures, showing traces of premodern shrine-temple syncretism.
[[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]]

[[JA:加佐美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398477}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=加佐美神社 | 画像=[[File:Kasami jinja buden.JPG|270px|拝殿]]
加佐美神社拝殿（2011年2月） | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|各務原市|Q641491}}{{jalink|蘇原古市場町|Q131870109}}五丁目1番地 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=加佐美大神
{{jalink|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|Q3488899}}
{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}} | 創建={{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}6年（{{jalink|864年|Q25041}}） | 本殿=三間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 例祭={{jalink|10月14日|Q2922}}・{{jalink|10月15日|Q2919|15日}} | 神事={{jalink|#主な神事}}を参照 | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=加佐美神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''加佐美神社'''（かさみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|各務原市|Q641491}}{{jalink|蘇原古市場町|Q131870109}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|新境川|Q11501642}}の左岸、加佐美山の東南東に位置する。 {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}である。元々は蘇原10カ村（{{jalink|伊飛島村|Q18990514|伊吹・飛鳥・島崎}}・{{jalink|和合村 (岐阜県稲葉郡)|Q23940976|野口・熊田・東島・坂井}}・{{jalink|大宮村 (岐阜県)|Q18748127|大島・宮代}}・{{jalink|古市場村 (岐阜県各務郡)|Q22125133|古市場}}）の{{jalink|総社|Q1107129}}であるが、現在は{{jalink|蘇原町|Q11625179|蘇原地区}}の総社、{{jalink|産土神|Q1392097}}とされている。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は郷社。 各務原市内の{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}五社のうちの一社である[https://www.tezikarao.org/news/detail.php?id=149 各務原市金幣五社巡り]、岐阜県神社庁各務原市支部。 === 概略 === {{jalink|864年|Q25041}}（{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}6年）、{{jalink|清和天皇|Q349243}}により創建。かつてこの地を{{jalink|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|Q3488899}}などの{{jalink|蘇我氏|Q909854}}が治めており、「蘇原」の地名は、蘇我氏の領有に由来するといわれている。蘇我倉山田石川麻呂が亡くなった後、曽原荘（現在の蘇原）の人々が蘇我倉山田石川麻呂を崇敬し、祭祀したのが始まりといわれており、神社自体は{{jalink|7世紀|Q8089}}には存在した可能性がある。{{jalink|905年|Q24239}}（{{jalink|延喜|Q1374325}}5年）ころ、{{jalink|各務郡|Q11412369}}曽原荘古市場にあり八幡宮と呼ばれていたという。{{jalink|908年|Q30463}}（延喜8年）には笠見明神と称されたという。なお、{{jalink|1912年|Q2077}}（明治45年）発行の『{{jalink|府県郷社明治神社誌料|Q11486103|府縣郷社明治神社誌料}}』中巻によれば、{{jalink|祭神|Q11591100}}及び創建年は不詳としている{{Cite journal |和書 |title=岐阜縣之部 |journal={{jalink|府県郷社明治神社誌料|Q11486103|府縣郷社明治神社誌料}} |volume=中巻 |publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所 |date=1912-01-15 |pages=36-37 |doi=10.11501/1088278}}。 『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156|美濃國}}各務郡七座のうちに「加佐美神社」とあり{{Cite journal |和書 |title=延喜式 卷十 神祇十 神名下 |journal={{jalink|国史大系|Q10926780|國史大系}} |volume=13 |publisher={{jalink|経済雑誌社|Q11606486}} |date=1900-11-15 |pages=346 |doi=10.11501/991103}}、『美濃國神名帳』では各務郡座二十三社のうちに「{{jalink|正四位|Q11123338|正四位下}}笠見田明神」と記されている{{Cite journal |和書 |editor={{jalink|佐伯有義|Q11382847}} 等 |title=延暦寺本美濃国神名帳 |journal=神祇全書 |volume=第4輯 |publisher={{jalink|皇典講究所|Q11580997}} |date=1908-05-17 |pages=624 |doi=10.11501/991328}}{{Cite journal |和書 |editor=各務原市教育委員会 |title=第二節 延喜式神名帳にみる神社 |journal=各務原市史 |volume=通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=各務原市 |date=1986-12-20 |pages=626-630 |doi=10.11501/9571788}}。なお、『各務原市史』では「『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』貞観6年（864年）8月15日{{jalink|己巳|Q1062039}}条には「{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|従五位|Q11071125|従五位下}}加佐美神授ク二従五位上ヲ一」とみえる」と記すが{{Efn|{{jalink|引用|Q206287}}に際して{{jalink|漢数字|Q683566}}を{{jalink|アラビア数字|Q29961325}}に変更。}}、写本によっては記載が異なる{{Efn|{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}年間{{jalink|松下見林|Q92923373}}校正本を底本とする{{jalink|1914年|Q2083}}（{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}3年）発行の『日本三代実録』では「美濃国」とするが{{Cite journal |和書 |title=三代実録卷九 清和天皇（貞觀六年八月） |journal=日本三代實録 |publisher=経済雑誌社 |date=1914-02-28 |pages=184 |quote=十五日己巳。美濃國従五位下長田神。免上神田神。加佐美神。（中略）並授二従五位上一 |doi=10.11501/950691}}、{{jalink|宮内省|Q8193428}}{{jalink|図書寮|Q11420348}}所蔵谷森健男氏旧蔵本を底本とする{{jalink|1944年|Q5268}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}19年）発行の『日本三代実録』では「{{jalink|美作国|Q936036}}」とする{{Cite journal |和書 |title=三代実録卷九 清和天皇（貞觀六年八月） |journal=日本三代實録 |publisher=大八洲出版創立事務所 |date=1944-06-28 |pages=140 |quote=十五日己巳。美作國従五位下長田神。免上神田神。前社神。（中略）並授二従五位上一 |doi=10.11501/3456452}}。}}。 境内には{{jalink|阿弥陀堂|Q11657414}}、{{jalink|薬師堂|Q11622438}}があり、{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}の名残がある。かつては{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}として根本寺が存在したが、明治初期に{{jalink|廃仏毀釈|Q702298}}で{{jalink|廃寺|Q11486287}}となっている。本殿（{{jalink|1687年|Q7668}}建築）、幣殿（{{jalink|1910年|Q2075}}建築）、拝殿（{{jalink|1746年|Q7589}}建築）は、{{jalink|2008|Q1069941}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}20年）4月18日に国の{{jalink|登録有形文化財|Q11579194}}に登録されている{{Cite web |和書 |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami3.html |title=加佐美神社本殿 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami3.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{文化遺産オンライン|144291|加佐美神社本殿}}{{Cite web |和書 |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami2.html |title=加佐美神社幣殿 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami2.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{文化遺産オンライン|172332|加佐美神社幣殿}}{{Cite web |和書 |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami1.html |title=加佐美神社拝殿 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami1.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{文化遺産オンライン|186933|加佐美神社拝殿}}『式内金幣社加佐美神社の栞』。 蘇我氏とつながりが深く、周辺には蘇我石川麻呂の墳墓（{{jalink|伝蘇我倉山田石川麻呂の墓|Q11381424}}）や、山田寺跡（{{jalink|山田寺塔心礎|Q11470319}}）がある。 {{jalink|1975年|Q2479}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}50年）9月16日、岐阜県神社長指定第76号により金幣社に指定される{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=780 |title=加佐美神社 |publisher=岐阜県神社庁 |accessdate=2025-05-09}}。 {{jalink|参道|Q667783}}は{{jalink|各務原市#市内の道路通称名|Q641491|蘇原中央通り}}の一部に該当し、{{jalink|御旅所|Q11489117}}は参道沿いの蘇原古市場町二丁目にある。かつては参道には二ノ鳥居と三ノ鳥居があり、二ノ鳥居は蘇原中央通りと{{jalink|各務原市#市内の道路通称名|Q641491|おがせ街道}}との交点、三ノ鳥居は蘇原中央通りと{{jalink|各務原市#市内の道路通称名|Q641491|山田寺通り}}との交点に存在したといわれている{{Cite book |和書 |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/008/305/03_kasami240815.pdf |title=各務原市 地区別景観計画 風景形成基準ガイドライン 重点風景地区 3 加佐美神社地区 |format=PDF |publisher=各務原市 |page=1 |accessdate=2025-02-26}}。 かつて加佐美神社には一之宮、二之宮、三之宮があり、一之宮は各務郡古市場村（現・蘇原古市場町）の{{jalink|神明神社 (各務原市蘇原古市場町)|Q134987025|神明神社}}、二之宮は各務郡伊吹村（現・{{jalink|蘇原伊吹町|Q131900799}}）の{{jalink|八坂神社 (各務原市蘇原伊吹町)|Q134928112|八坂神社}}、三之宮は各務郡野口村（現・{{jalink|蘇原野口町|Q131870111}}）の熊野神社{{Efn|{{jalink|1909年|Q2057}}（明治42年）、{{jalink|神明神社 (各務原市蘇原野口町)|Q134926762|神明神社}}（{{jalink|蘇原野口町|Q131870111}}）に合祀。}}である現地解説板「加佐美神社「二の鳥居」の台石」より。各務原市{{jalink|景観条例|Q11514689|都市景観条例}}に基づき、{{jalink|2008年|Q2004}}（平成20年）8月1日に各務原市告示第80号で神社を含む周辺の約37.29{{jalink|ヘクタール|Q35852}}が重点風景地区に指定されている{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/shisei/shisaku/1008207/1008297/1008302/1008305.html |title=重点風景地区（決定） 歴史的資源・風致を有する地区 |publisher=各務原市 |date=2024-08-16 |accessdate=2025-05-09}}。 {{jalink|戦国時代 (日本)|Q204023|戦国時代}}には加佐美神社は{{jalink|八幡宮|Q11390939}}と呼ばれ、神社の裏山の加佐美山も八幡山と呼ばれており、{{jalink|八幡山城 (美濃国)|Q38276751|八幡山城}}が存在した。 === 祭神 === * 加佐美大神 ** 一説によると、かつては加佐美神社の西北西にある「加佐美山」自体が信仰の対象であったという。加佐美大神は加佐美山を神としたと推測される。 * {{jalink|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|Q3488899}} * {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} === 境内社 === * 秋葉神社（{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|迦遇槌神}}） * 御鍬神社（{{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|豊受大神}}） * 加佐美稲荷神社（{{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|倉稲魂命}}） * 神明神社（{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}） === その他 === * {{jalink|庚申塔|Q11486001}} ** かつての加佐美神社の二の鳥居の礎石の一つを使用し、{{jalink|1980年|Q2439}}（昭和55年）建立現地解説板「加佐美神社庚申塔について」より。 * {{jalink|忠魂碑|Q11274518}}（{{jalink|日清戦争|Q178687|征清紀念碑}}、{{jalink|日露戦争|Q159950|日露戦役}}忠魂碑、殉国戦士碑） ** 元は別の場所に設置されていたのを境内に移転。 === 主な神事 === 『式内金幣社加佐美神社の栞』による。 * 1月1日 - {{jalink|歳旦祭|Q11546824}}・開運祈禱祭・交通安全祭 * 2月節分 - {{jalink|節分|Q746798}}祭 * 旧暦2月14日 - 秋葉祭 * 2月20日 - {{jalink|祈年祭|Q118906710}}・御鍬祭 * 3月15日 - 住吉祭 * 4月第一土曜日 - 奥之宮祭・水神祭 * 7月最終日曜日 - {{jalink|大祓|Q11261910}}神事 * 10月15日以前の第二日曜日 - {{jalink|例祭|Q11385469|例大祭}} ** かつては毎年10月14日・15日に行われていた。花御輿が繰り出される。以前はけんか神輿が奉納されており、事故で一時中断していたが、{{jalink|蘇原六軒町|Q131900816}}を中心とする「六軒みこしの会」により、けんか神輿を模擬演舞として復活している{{Cite web |和書 |url=https://rokken-mikoshi.jimdosite.com/ |title=六軒みこしの会 |accessdate=2025-05-09}}{{Cite news |和書 |url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/791540 |title=垂直に立て「けんかみこし」 各務原加佐美神社で例大祭 |newspaper={{jalink|中日新聞|Q904678}} |date=2023-10-19}}。 * 11月15日 - {{jalink|七五三|Q1072387}}祭 * 11月23日 - 新穀感謝祭 * 12月31日 - {{jalink|除夜|Q7245702}}祭 * 毎月1日・15日 - 月次祭 === 文化財 === * 木造狛犬 ** 高さ約115cmの木製の{{jalink|狛犬|Q20155846}}。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}前期の製作と推測される。市指定文化財（{{jalink|1974年|Q2478}}（昭和49年）6月26日）{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=市指定文化財 |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=各務原市}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005217.html |title=実は「狛犬」は片方だけ？優美な木造の彫刻 |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=各務原市}}。 * 加佐美神社獅子頭 ** {{jalink|延宝|Q1145735}}3年（{{jalink|1675年|Q7624}}）各務郡野口村から寄進された木造の獅子頭。10月の例大祭の神幸行列では、獅子頭が神輿の行列を先導する。市指定文化財（{{jalink|1970年|Q2474}}（昭和45年）3月23日）{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005225.html |title=「おししさま」の伝承が伝わる地域の宝 |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=各務原市}}。 === 所在地 === * {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|各務原市|Q641491}}{{jalink|蘇原古市場町|Q131870109}}五丁目1番地 === 交通機関 === * {{jalink|岐阜乗合自動車|Q5560101|岐阜バス}}尾崎団地線「蘇原」{{jalink|バス停留所|Q953806|バス停}}下車、徒歩で約10分。 ** {{jalink|JR岐阜駅バスターミナル|Q11226229}}13番のりば「諏訪山団地」「{{jalink|岐阜県立各務原高等学校|Q11471490|各務原高校}}前」「{{jalink|テクノプラザ (岐阜県)|Q11319203|テクノプラザ}}」「{{jalink|三柿野駅|Q6845663}}」行きに乗車。 ** {{jalink|名鉄岐阜のりば|Q11415771}}6番のりば「諏訪山団地」「各務原高校前」「テクノプラザ」「三柿野駅」行に乗車。 * {{jalink|各務原市ふれあいバス|Q11412316}} ** 蘇原線「蘇原古市場町」バス停下車、徒歩で約8分。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} [[JA:加佐美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398477}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name=Kasami Shrine | Image=[[File:Kasami jinja buden.JPG|270px|Oratory]]
Kasami Shrine oratory (February 2011) | Address={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara City|Q641491}}{{jalink|Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109}} 5-chome 1 | location= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = Kasami Okami
{{jalink|Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} | Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} | Founded={{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}}6 years ({{jalink|864 years|Q25041}}) | Main shrine = Sangensha {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} | Annual festival={{jalink|October 14th|Q2922}}・{{jalink|October 15th|Q2919|15th}} |Refer to Shinto rituals={{jalink|#Main rituals}}| Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kasami Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kasami Shrine'' (Kasami Shrine) is {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara City|Q641491}}{{jalink|Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109}}. Located on the left bank of {{jalink|Shinsakaigawa|Q11501642}}, east-southeast of Mt. Kasami.{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shikiuchisha}}. Originally 10 villages of Sohara ({{jalink|Ibishima Village|Q18990514|Ibuki, Asuka, Shimazaki}}, {{jalink|Wago Village (Inaba District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q23940976|Noguchi, Kumada, Higashijima, Sakai}}, {{jalink|Omiya Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q18748127|Oshima/Miyashiro}}・{{jalink|Furuichi Market Village (Kakamami District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q22125133|Furuichi Market}}) is {{jalink|Soja|Q1107129}}, but it is currently considered to be the Sojajin of {{jalink|Sohara Town|Q11625179|Sohara District}} and {{jalink|Udojin|Q1392097}}. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is a Gosha. It is one of the {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} five shrines in Kakamigahara City, [https://www.tezikarao.org/news/detail.php?id=149 Kakamigahara City Kinpei Five Shrines Tour], Gifu Prefectural Shrine Agency Kakamigahara City Branch. === Summary ==={{jalink|864|Q25041}} ({{jalink|Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jokan}}6), founded by {{jalink|Emperor Seiwa|Q349243}}. In the past, this area was ruled by {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}} such as {{jalink|Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899}}, and the place name ``Sohara'' is said to have come from the Soga clan's possession. It is said that after Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro passed away, the people of Sohara Manor (currently Sohara) worshiped him and held rituals there, and the shrine itself may have existed in the {{jalink|7th century|Q8089}}. It is said that around {{jalink|905|Q24239}} ({{jalink|Enki|Q1374325}}5), it was located in {{jalink|Kakamami District|Q11412369}} Soharasho Old Market and was called Hachiman Shrine. It is said that it was called Kasami Myojin in {{jalink|908|Q30463}} (Engi 8). Furthermore, according to the middle volume of "{{jalink|Prefectural Gosha Meiji Shrine Journals|Q11486103|Fugata Gosha Meiji Shrine Journals}}" published {{jalink|1912|Q2077}} (Meiji 45), the {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} and the year of its founding are unknown{{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Gifu Prefecture |journal={{jalink|Prefecture Gosha Meiji Shrine Magazine |Q11486103|Prefectural Gosha Meiji Shrine Magazine}} |volume=Volume 2 |publisher=Meiji Shrine Magazine |date=1912-01-15 |pages=36-37 |doi=10.11501/1088278}}.In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, there is "Kasami Shrine" in the seven temples of Kagami District in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156|Minokuni}} Kaminashita |journal={{jalink|Kokushi Taikei|Q10926780|Kokushi Taikei}} |volume=13 |publisher={{jalink|Keizai Zasshisha|Q11606486}} |date=1900-11-15 |pages=346 |doi=10.11501/991103}}, "Minokuni Shinmecho" lists "{{jalink|Senior Fourth Rank|Q11123338|Senior Fourth Rank Lower}}Kasamita Myojin" among the 23 shrines in Kagami-gunza{{Cite journal |Japanese book |editor={{jalink|Yiyoshi Saeki|Q11382847}} etc. |title=Enryakuji Hon Mino Province Shinmecho |journal=Jingi Zensho |volume=Volume 4 |publisher={{jalink|Koten Kokyujo|Q11580997}} |date=1908-05-17 |pages=624 |doi=10.11501/991328}}{{Cite journal |Japanese book |editor=Kakamigahara City Board of Education |title=Section 2 Shrines as seen in the Engishiki Shinmyocho |journal=Kakamigahara City History |volume=General history Nature, primitive, ancient, medieval |publisher=Kakamigahara City |date=1986-12-20 |pages=626-630 |doi=10.11501/9571788}}. In addition, in the "Kakamigahara City History", "{{jalink|Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981}}" August 15, 864 {{jalink|Kimi|Q1062 039}}Article ``{{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}}{{jalink|Junior Fifth Rank|Q11071125|Junior Fifth Rank Lower}}Kasami Kamishōku TwoJunior Fifth Rank UpperwoOne'', but at the time of {{Efn|{{jalink|quote|Q206287}}, {{jalink|Chinese numerals|Q683566}} was changed to {{jalink|Arabic numerals|Q29961325}}. }}, the description varies depending on the manuscript. In the ``Nihon Sandai Jiroku'' published in {{jalink|1914|Q2083}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}}3), which is based on ``Mino Province'', {{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Third Dynasty Actual Record Volume 9 Emperor Seiwa (August 6th year of Teikō) |journal=Japan's Third Dynasty Actual Record |publisher=Keizai Zasshisha |date=1914-02-28 |pages=184 |quote=15th Kimimi. Mino Province Junior Fifth Rank Shimonagata no Kami. Mengami Kanda God. Kasamikami. (omitted) Parallel class2Junior Fifth Rank Upper1 {{j alink|1944|Q5268}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 19th year) "Japan Three Generations Jitsuroku" says "{{jalink|Mimasakakuni|Q936036}}"{{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Third Dynasty Actual Record Volume 9 Emperor Seiwa (August 1987) |journal=Japan's Three Dynasty Actual Record |publisher=Daiyashu Publishing Founding Office |date=1944-06-28 |pages=140 |quote=15th Kimi. Mimasakakuni Jugoi Shimonagata no Kami. Mengami Kanda God. Former shrine deity. (omitted) Parallel class 2Junior Fifth Rank Upper 1 |doi=10.11501/3456452}}. }}.In the precincts there are {{jalink|Amida-do|Q11657414}} and {{jalink|Yakushi-do|Q11622438}}, and there are remnants of {{jalink|Shinto-Buddhist syncretism|Q2279088}}. Nemotoji once existed as {{jalink|Jinguji|Q1074971}}, but in the early Meiji period, it became {{jalink|Abandoned Temple|Q11486287}} in {{jalink|Haibutsu Kishaku|Q702298}}. The main shrine (built in {{jalink|1687|Q7668}}), the shrine (built in {{jalink|1910|Q2075}}), and the worship hall (built in {{jalink|1746|Q7589}}) are |2008|Q1069941}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}20) Registered as a national {{jalink|registered tangible cultural property|Q11579194}} on April 18th {{Cite web |Japanese book |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami3.html |title=Kasami Shrine Main Hall |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami3.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{Cultural Heritage Online|144291|Kasami Shrine Main Hall}}{{Cite web |Japanese books |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami2.html |title=Kasami Shrine Heiden |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami2.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{Cultural Heritage Online|172332|Kasami Shrine Heideden}}{{Cite web |Japanese books |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami1.html |title=Kasami Shrine Hall of Worship |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami1.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{Cultural Heritage Online|186933|Kasami Shrine Worship Hall}}``Shikiuchi Kinpeisha Kasami Shrine Bookmark''.It has a deep connection with the Soga clan, and in the surrounding area there are the tomb of Soga Ishikawamaro ({{jalink|legendary Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro's tomb|Q11381424}}) and the ruins of Yamada-dera Temple ({{jalink|Yamada-dera Pagoda Heart Foundation|Q11470319}}). {{jalink|1975|Q2479}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}50th year) On September 16th, it was designated as Kinpeisha by Gifu Prefecture Shinto President Designation No. 76{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=780 |title=Kasami Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefectural Shrine Agency |accessdate=2025-05-09}}.{{jalink|Sando|Q667783}} corresponds to a part of {{jalink|Kakamigahara City#City Road Common Name|Q641491|Sohara Chuo-dori}}, and {{jalink|Otabisho|Q11489117}} is located along the approach to Sohara Furuichiba-cho 2-chome. In the past, there were two torii gates and three torii gates on the approach to the shrine, and the latter gate was connected to Sowara Chuo Dori and {{jalink|Kakamigahara City#Common names of roads in the city|Q641491|Ogase Kaido}}. The intersection, Sannotorii, is said to have existed at the intersection of Sowara Chuo-dori Street and {{jalink|Kakamigahara City#Common name for roads in the city|Q641491|Yamada-dera Street}}{{Cite book |Japanese book |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/008/305/03_kasami240815.pdf |title=Kakamigahara City District Landscape Plan Landscape Formation Standards Guidelines Key Scenic District 3 Kasami Shrine District |format=PDF |publisher=Kakamigahara City |page=1 |accessdate=2025-02-26}}.Kasami Shrine once had Ichinomiya, Ninomiya, and Sannomiya, and Ichinomiya was the Shinmei Shrine in Furuichiba Village, Kagami District (now Sohara Furuichiba Town). (Kakamigahara City, Sohara Furuichiba Town)|Q134987025|Shinmei Shrine}}, Ninomiya is {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine} in Ibuki Village, Kakamiga District (currently {{jalink|Sohara Ibuki Town|Q131900799}}) (Sohara Ibuki-cho, Kakamigahara City)|Q134928112|Yasaka Shrine}}, Sannomiya is Kumano Shrine {{Efn|{{jalink|1909|Q2057}} (Meiji 42), {{jalink|Shinmei Shrine (Sohara Noguchi Town, Kakamigahara City)|Q134926762|Shinmei Shrine}} ({{jalink|Sohara Noguchi Town|Q131870111}}). }}From the local commentary board ``Kasami Shrine "Nino Torii" base stone''. Based on the Kakamigahara City {{jalink|Landscape Ordinance|Q11514689|Urban Landscape Ordinance}}, on August 1, 2008|Q2004, Kakamigahara City Notification No. 80 designated approximately 37.29{{jalink|ha|Q35852}} surrounding the shrine as a key scenic area.{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/shisei/shisaku/1008207/1008297/1008302/1008305.html |title=Important scenic areas (determined) Districts with historical resources and scenery |publisher=Kakamigahara City |date=2024-08-16 |accessdate=2025-05-09}}.During the {{jalink|Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|Sengoku period}}, Kasami Shrine was called {{jalink|Hachimangu|Q11390939}}, and Mt. Kasami on the mountain behind the shrine was also called Mt. Hachiman, and {{jalink|Hachimanyama Castle (Mino Province)|Q38276751|Hachimanyama Castle}} === Enshrined God === * Kasami Okami ** According to one theory, ``Kasamiyama'' itself, located to the west-northwest of Kasami Shrine, was once an object of worship. It is assumed that Kasami Okami considered Mt. Kasami as a god. * {{jalink|Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899}} * {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} === Precinct Company === * Akiba Shrine ({{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Katsuchijin}}) * Okuwa Shrine ({{jalink|Toyoukebime|Q2983724|Toyouke Okami}}) * Kasami Inari Shrine ({{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Kuraina Tama no Mikoto}}) * Shinmei Shrine ({{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602}}) === Others === * {{jalink|Koshin Tower|Q11486001}} ** Built in {{jalink|1980|Q2439}} (1980) using one of the cornerstones of the former second torii gate of Kasami Shrine. * {{jalink|Loyalty Monument|Q11274518}} ({{jalink|Sino-Japanese War|Q178687|Conquering and Qing Monument}}, {{jalink|Russo-Japanese War|Q159950|Russo-Japanese War}} Loyalty Monument, Martyred Soldiers Monument) ** It was originally installed in a different location, but was moved to the shrine grounds. === Main rituals === Based on "Shikiuchi Kinpeisha Kasami Shrine Bookmark". * January 1st - {{jalink|Saidan Festival|Q11546824}}, Good Luck Prayer Festival, Traffic Safety Festival * February Setsubun - {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798}} Festival * February 14th of the lunar calendar - Akiba Festival * February 20th - {{jalink|prayer festival|Q118906710}}・Okuwa Festival * March 15th - Sumiyoshi Festival * First Saturday of April - Okunomiya Festival/Suijin Festival * Last Sunday of July - {{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910}} Shinto ritual * Second Sunday before October 15th - {{jalink|Reitaisai|Q11385469|Reitaisai}} ** It used to be held on October 14th and 15th every year. A flower mikoshi is carried out. Previously, the Kenka Mikoshi was dedicated, but it was temporarily suspended due to an accident, but the "Rokken Mikoshi Association" centered around {{jalink|Sohara Rokkencho|Q131900816}} has revived the Kenka Mikoshi as a mock performance.{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://rokken-mikoshi.jimdosite.com/ |title=Rokuken Mikoshi Association |accessdate=2025-05-09}}{{Cite news |Japanese book |url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/791540 |title=Vertically ``Fighting Mikoshi'' Regular festival at Kakamigahara Kasami Shrine |newspaper={{jalink|Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678}} |date=2023-10-19}}.* November 15th - {{jalink|Shichigosan|Q1072387}} Festival * November 23rd - New Grain Thanksgiving * December 31st - {{jalink|New Year's Eve|Q7245702}} Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Monthly Festival === Cultural assets === * Wooden guardian dog ** A wooden {{jalink|guardian dog|Q20155846}} approximately 115cm tall. It is thought to have been produced in the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} period. City-designated cultural property ({{jalink|1974|Q2478}} (June 26, 1974)) {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-designated cultural property |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005217.html |title=Is there actually only one “guardian dog”? Elegant wooden sculpture |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}. * Kasami Shrine Lion Head** A wooden lion head donated by Noguchi Village, Kagami District in {{jalink|Enpo|Q1145735}}3 ({{jalink|1675|Q7624}}). During the annual shrine festival procession in October, a lion head leads the mikoshi procession. City-designated cultural property ({{jalink|1970|Q2474}} (March 23, 1974)) {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005225.html |title=A regional treasure where the legend of "Oshishi-sama" is passed down |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}. === Location === * {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara City|Q641491}}{{jalink|Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109}} 5-chome 1 === Transportation === * {{jalink|Gifu Shared Car|Q5560101|Gifu Bus}} Get off at Ozaki Danchi Line "Sohara" {{jalink|Bus Stop|Q953806|Bus Stop}} and walk for about 10 minutes. ** {{jalink|JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal|Q11226229}} Platform 13 "Suwayama Danchi" "{{jalink|Gifu Prefectural Kakamigahara High School|Q11471490|Kakamigahara High School}}" "{{jalink|Techno Plaza (Gifu Prefecture)|Q11319203|Techno Plaza}}" and "{{jalink|Mikakino Station|Q6845663}}". ** {{jalink|Meitetsu Gifu Bus Stop|Q11415771}} Take the bus bound for ``Suwayama Danchi,'' ``Kakamigahara High School,'' ``Techno Plaza,'' and ``Mikakino Station'' at bus stop 6. * {{jalink|Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316}} ** Get off at Sohara Line "Sohara Furuichibacho" bus stop and walk for about 8 minutes. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Terikio Shrine (Kakamigahara City)|Q11496922}} * {{jalink|Murakuni Shrine|Q11523055}} * {{jalink|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|Q11523053}} * {{jalink|Mii Shrine (Kakamigahara City)|Q11488771}} * {{jalink|List of registered tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture|Q119930208}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Kasami-jinja}} * {{Instagram|kasami_jinjya_|Kasami Shrine}} * {{kotobank}} * [https://kakamigahara-kankou.jp/tourism/256 Kasami Shrine｜Kakamigahara Walk (Kakamigahara City Tourist Information)] * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_7128.html Kasami Shrine | Tourist spots | Gifu Prefecture Tourism Official Website "Gifu Travel Guide"] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kasami Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kasami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kasami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kasami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] {{Expand Japanese|加佐美神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kasami Shrine | native_name = 加佐美神社 | image = Kasami jinja buden.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Kasami Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.422628|136.865485}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Kasami Shrine'''|加佐美神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो Mino|ru|Мино (провинция)|simple|Mino Province|sk|Mino (provincia)|sr|Провинција Мино|th|แคว้นมิโนะ|tr|Mino (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Міно|vi|Tỉnh Kasami jinja buden.JPG == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181947 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|lt=Q135159299|WD=Q135159299}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|WD=Q3029647}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == name in kana (P1814) == * かさみじんじゃ == Instagram username (P2003) == * kasami_jinjya_ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6200005002362 == Commons category (P373) == * Kasami-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県各務原市蘇原古市場町五丁目1番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kasami-jinja}} [[JA:加佐美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398477}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name=Kasami Shrine | Image=[[File:Kasami jinja buden.JPG|270px|Oratory]]
Kasami Shrine oratory (February 2011) | Address={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara City|Q641491}}{{jalink|Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109}} 5-chome 1 | location= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = Kasami Okami
{{jalink|Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} | Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} | Founded={{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}}6 years ({{jalink|864 years|Q25041}}) | Main shrine = Sangensha {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} | Annual festival={{jalink|October 14th|Q2922}}・{{jalink|October 15th|Q2919|15th}} |Refer to Shinto rituals={{jalink|#Main rituals}}| Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kasami Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kasami Shrine'' (Kasami Shrine) is {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara City|Q641491}}{{jalink|Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109}}. Located on the left bank of {{jalink|Shinsakaigawa|Q11501642}}, east-southeast of Mt. Kasami.{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shikiuchisha}}. Originally 10 villages of Sohara ({{jalink|Ibishima Village|Q18990514|Ibuki, Asuka, Shimazaki}}, {{jalink|Wago Village (Inaba District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q23940976|Noguchi, Kumada, Higashijima, Sakai}}, {{jalink|Omiya Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q18748127|Oshima/Miyashiro}}・{{jalink|Furuichi Market Village (Kakamami District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q22125133|Furuichi Market}}) is {{jalink|Soja|Q1107129}}, but it is currently considered to be the Sojajin of {{jalink|Sohara Town|Q11625179|Sohara District}} and {{jalink|Udojin|Q1392097}}. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is a Gosha. It is one of the {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} five shrines in Kakamigahara City, [https://www.tezikarao.org/news/detail.php?id=149 Kakamigahara City Kinpei Five Shrines Tour], Gifu Prefectural Shrine Agency Kakamigahara City Branch. === Summary ==={{jalink|864|Q25041}} ({{jalink|Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jokan}}6), founded by {{jalink|Emperor Seiwa|Q349243}}. In the past, this area was ruled by {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}} such as {{jalink|Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899}}, and the place name ``Sohara'' is said to have come from the Soga clan's possession. It is said that after Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro passed away, the people of Sohara Manor (currently Sohara) worshiped him and held rituals there, and the shrine itself may have existed in the {{jalink|7th century|Q8089}}. It is said that around {{jalink|905|Q24239}} ({{jalink|Enki|Q1374325}}5), it was located in {{jalink|Kakamami District|Q11412369}} Soharasho Old Market and was called Hachiman Shrine. It is said that it was called Kasami Myojin in {{jalink|908|Q30463}} (Engi 8). Furthermore, according to the middle volume of "{{jalink|Prefectural Gosha Meiji Shrine Journals|Q11486103|Fugata Gosha Meiji Shrine Journals}}" published {{jalink|1912|Q2077}} (Meiji 45), the {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} and the year of its founding are unknown{{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Gifu Prefecture |journal={{jalink|Prefecture Gosha Meiji Shrine Magazine |Q11486103|Prefectural Gosha Meiji Shrine Magazine}} |volume=Volume 2 |publisher=Meiji Shrine Magazine |date=1912-01-15 |pages=36-37 |doi=10.11501/1088278}}.In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, there is "Kasami Shrine" in the seven temples of Kagami District in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156|Minokuni}} Kaminashita |journal={{jalink|Kokushi Taikei|Q10926780|Kokushi Taikei}} |volume=13 |publisher={{jalink|Keizai Zasshisha|Q11606486}} |date=1900-11-15 |pages=346 |doi=10.11501/991103}}, "Minokuni Shinmecho" lists "{{jalink|Senior Fourth Rank|Q11123338|Senior Fourth Rank Lower}}Kasamita Myojin" among the 23 shrines in Kagami-gunza{{Cite journal |Japanese book |editor={{jalink|Yiyoshi Saeki|Q11382847}} etc. |title=Enryakuji Hon Mino Province Shinmecho |journal=Jingi Zensho |volume=Volume 4 |publisher={{jalink|Koten Kokyujo|Q11580997}} |date=1908-05-17 |pages=624 |doi=10.11501/991328}}{{Cite journal |Japanese book |editor=Kakamigahara City Board of Education |title=Section 2 Shrines as seen in the Engishiki Shinmyocho |journal=Kakamigahara City History |volume=General history Nature, primitive, ancient, medieval |publisher=Kakamigahara City |date=1986-12-20 |pages=626-630 |doi=10.11501/9571788}}. In addition, in the "Kakamigahara City History", "{{jalink|Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981}}" August 15, 864 {{jalink|Kimi|Q1062 039}}Article ``{{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}}{{jalink|Junior Fifth Rank|Q11071125|Junior Fifth Rank Lower}}Kasami Kamishōku TwoJunior Fifth Rank UpperwoOne'', but at the time of {{Efn|{{jalink|quote|Q206287}}, {{jalink|Chinese numerals|Q683566}} was changed to {{jalink|Arabic numerals|Q29961325}}. }}, the description varies depending on the manuscript. In the ``Nihon Sandai Jiroku'' published in {{jalink|1914|Q2083}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}}3), which is based on ``Mino Province'', {{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Third Dynasty Actual Record Volume 9 Emperor Seiwa (August 6th year of Teikō) |journal=Japan's Third Dynasty Actual Record |publisher=Keizai Zasshisha |date=1914-02-28 |pages=184 |quote=15th Kimimi. Mino Province Junior Fifth Rank Shimonagata no Kami. Mengami Kanda God. Kasamikami. (omitted) Parallel class2Junior Fifth Rank Upper1 {{j alink|1944|Q5268}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 19th year) "Japan Three Generations Jitsuroku" says "{{jalink|Mimasakakuni|Q936036}}"{{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Third Dynasty Actual Record Volume 9 Emperor Seiwa (August 1987) |journal=Japan's Three Dynasty Actual Record |publisher=Daiyashu Publishing Founding Office |date=1944-06-28 |pages=140 |quote=15th Kimi. Mimasakakuni Jugoi Shimonagata no Kami. Mengami Kanda God. Former shrine deity. (omitted) Parallel class 2Junior Fifth Rank Upper 1 |doi=10.11501/3456452}}. }}.In the precincts there are {{jalink|Amida-do|Q11657414}} and {{jalink|Yakushi-do|Q11622438}}, and there are remnants of {{jalink|Shinto-Buddhist syncretism|Q2279088}}. Nemotoji once existed as {{jalink|Jinguji|Q1074971}}, but in the early Meiji period, it became {{jalink|Abandoned Temple|Q11486287}} in {{jalink|Haibutsu Kishaku|Q702298}}. The main shrine (built in {{jalink|1687|Q7668}}), the shrine (built in {{jalink|1910|Q2075}}), and the worship hall (built in {{jalink|1746|Q7589}}) are |2008|Q1069941}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}20) Registered as a national {{jalink|registered tangible cultural property|Q11579194}} on April 18th {{Cite web |Japanese book |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami3.html |title=Kasami Shrine Main Hall |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami3.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{Cultural Heritage Online|144291|Kasami Shrine Main Hall}}{{Cite web |Japanese books |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami2.html |title=Kasami Shrine Heiden |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami2.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{Cultural Heritage Online|172332|Kasami Shrine Heideden}}{{Cite web |Japanese books |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami1.html |title=Kasami Shrine Hall of Worship |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami1.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{Cultural Heritage Online|186933|Kasami Shrine Worship Hall}}``Shikiuchi Kinpeisha Kasami Shrine Bookmark''.It has a deep connection with the Soga clan, and in the surrounding area there are the tomb of Soga Ishikawamaro ({{jalink|legendary Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro's tomb|Q11381424}}) and the ruins of Yamada-dera Temple ({{jalink|Yamada-dera Pagoda Heart Foundation|Q11470319}}). {{jalink|1975|Q2479}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}50th year) On September 16th, it was designated as Kinpeisha by Gifu Prefecture Shinto President Designation No. 76{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=780 |title=Kasami Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefectural Shrine Agency |accessdate=2025-05-09}}.{{jalink|Sando|Q667783}} corresponds to a part of {{jalink|Kakamigahara City#City Road Common Name|Q641491|Sohara Chuo-dori}}, and {{jalink|Otabisho|Q11489117}} is located along the approach to Sohara Furuichiba-cho 2-chome. In the past, there were two torii gates and three torii gates on the approach to the shrine, and the latter gate was connected to Sowara Chuo Dori and {{jalink|Kakamigahara City#Common names of roads in the city|Q641491|Ogase Kaido}}. The intersection, Sannotorii, is said to have existed at the intersection of Sowara Chuo-dori Street and {{jalink|Kakamigahara City#Common name for roads in the city|Q641491|Yamada-dera Street}}{{Cite book |Japanese book |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/008/305/03_kasami240815.pdf |title=Kakamigahara City District Landscape Plan Landscape Formation Standards Guidelines Key Scenic District 3 Kasami Shrine District |format=PDF |publisher=Kakamigahara City |page=1 |accessdate=2025-02-26}}.Kasami Shrine once had Ichinomiya, Ninomiya, and Sannomiya, and Ichinomiya was the Shinmei Shrine in Furuichiba Village, Kagami District (now Sohara Furuichiba Town). (Kakamigahara City, Sohara Furuichiba Town)|Q134987025|Shinmei Shrine}}, Ninomiya is {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine} in Ibuki Village, Kakamiga District (currently {{jalink|Sohara Ibuki Town|Q131900799}}) (Sohara Ibuki-cho, Kakamigahara City)|Q134928112|Yasaka Shrine}}, Sannomiya is Kumano Shrine {{Efn|{{jalink|1909|Q2057}} (Meiji 42), {{jalink|Shinmei Shrine (Sohara Noguchi Town, Kakamigahara City)|Q134926762|Shinmei Shrine}} ({{jalink|Sohara Noguchi Town|Q131870111}}). }}From the local commentary board ``Kasami Shrine "Nino Torii" base stone''. Based on the Kakamigahara City {{jalink|Landscape Ordinance|Q11514689|Urban Landscape Ordinance}}, on August 1, 2008|Q2004, Kakamigahara City Notification No. 80 designated approximately 37.29{{jalink|ha|Q35852}} surrounding the shrine as a key scenic area.{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/shisei/shisaku/1008207/1008297/1008302/1008305.html |title=Important scenic areas (determined) Districts with historical resources and scenery |publisher=Kakamigahara City |date=2024-08-16 |accessdate=2025-05-09}}.During the {{jalink|Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|Sengoku period}}, Kasami Shrine was called {{jalink|Hachimangu|Q11390939}}, and Mt. Kasami on the mountain behind the shrine was also called Mt. Hachiman, and {{jalink|Hachimanyama Castle (Mino Province)|Q38276751|Hachimanyama Castle}} === Enshrined God === * Kasami Okami ** According to one theory, ``Kasamiyama'' itself, located to the west-northwest of Kasami Shrine, was once an object of worship. It is assumed that Kasami Okami considered Mt. Kasami as a god. * {{jalink|Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899}} * {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} === Precinct Company === * Akiba Shrine ({{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Katsuchijin}}) * Okuwa Shrine ({{jalink|Toyoukebime|Q2983724|Toyouke Okami}}) * Kasami Inari Shrine ({{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Kuraina Tama no Mikoto}}) * Shinmei Shrine ({{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602}}) === Others === * {{jalink|Koshin Tower|Q11486001}} ** Built in {{jalink|1980|Q2439}} (1980) using one of the cornerstones of the former second torii gate of Kasami Shrine. * {{jalink|Loyalty Monument|Q11274518}} ({{jalink|Sino-Japanese War|Q178687|Conquering and Qing Monument}}, {{jalink|Russo-Japanese War|Q159950|Russo-Japanese War}} Loyalty Monument, Martyred Soldiers Monument) ** It was originally installed in a different location, but was moved to the shrine grounds. === Main rituals === Based on "Shikiuchi Kinpeisha Kasami Shrine Bookmark". * January 1st - {{jalink|Saidan Festival|Q11546824}}, Good Luck Prayer Festival, Traffic Safety Festival * February Setsubun - {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798}} Festival * February 14th of the lunar calendar - Akiba Festival * February 20th - {{jalink|prayer festival|Q118906710}}・Okuwa Festival * March 15th - Sumiyoshi Festival * First Saturday of April - Okunomiya Festival/Suijin Festival * Last Sunday of July - {{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910}} Shinto ritual * Second Sunday before October 15th - {{jalink|Reitaisai|Q11385469|Reitaisai}} ** It used to be held on October 14th and 15th every year. A flower mikoshi is carried out. Previously, the Kenka Mikoshi was dedicated, but it was temporarily suspended due to an accident, but the "Rokken Mikoshi Association" centered around {{jalink|Sohara Rokkencho|Q131900816}} has revived the Kenka Mikoshi as a mock performance.{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://rokken-mikoshi.jimdosite.com/ |title=Rokuken Mikoshi Association |accessdate=2025-05-09}}{{Cite news |Japanese book |url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/791540 |title=Vertically ``Fighting Mikoshi'' Regular festival at Kakamigahara Kasami Shrine |newspaper={{jalink|Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678}} |date=2023-10-19}}.* November 15th - {{jalink|Shichigosan|Q1072387}} Festival * November 23rd - New Grain Thanksgiving * December 31st - {{jalink|New Year's Eve|Q7245702}} Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Monthly Festival === Cultural assets === * Wooden guardian dog ** A wooden {{jalink|guardian dog|Q20155846}} approximately 115cm tall. It is thought to have been produced in the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} period. City-designated cultural property ({{jalink|1974|Q2478}} (June 26, 1974)) {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-designated cultural property |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005217.html |title=Is there actually only one “guardian dog”? Elegant wooden sculpture |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}. * Kasami Shrine Lion Head** A wooden lion head donated by Noguchi Village, Kagami District in {{jalink|Enpo|Q1145735}}3 ({{jalink|1675|Q7624}}). During the annual shrine festival procession in October, a lion head leads the mikoshi procession. City-designated cultural property ({{jalink|1970|Q2474}} (March 23, 1974)) {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005225.html |title=A regional treasure where the legend of "Oshishi-sama" is passed down |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}. === Location === * {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara City|Q641491}}{{jalink|Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109}} 5-chome 1 === Transportation === * {{jalink|Gifu Shared Car|Q5560101|Gifu Bus}} Get off at Ozaki Danchi Line "Sohara" {{jalink|Bus Stop|Q953806|Bus Stop}} and walk for about 10 minutes. ** {{jalink|JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal|Q11226229}} Platform 13 "Suwayama Danchi" "{{jalink|Gifu Prefectural Kakamigahara High School|Q11471490|Kakamigahara High School}}" "{{jalink|Techno Plaza (Gifu Prefecture)|Q11319203|Techno Plaza}}" and "{{jalink|Mikakino Station|Q6845663}}". ** {{jalink|Meitetsu Gifu Bus Stop|Q11415771}} Take the bus bound for ``Suwayama Danchi,'' ``Kakamigahara High School,'' ``Techno Plaza,'' and ``Mikakino Station'' at bus stop 6. * {{jalink|Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316}} ** Get off at Sohara Line "Sohara Furuichibacho" bus stop and walk for about 8 minutes. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Terikio Shrine (Kakamigahara City)|Q11496922}} * {{jalink|Murakuni Shrine|Q11523055}} * {{jalink|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|Q11523053}} * {{jalink|Mii Shrine (Kakamigahara City)|Q11488771}} * {{jalink|List of registered tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture|Q119930208}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Kasami-jinja}} * {{Instagram|kasami_jinjya_|Kasami Shrine}} * {{kotobank}} * [https://kakamigahara-kankou.jp/tourism/256 Kasami Shrine｜Kakamigahara Walk (Kakamigahara City Tourist Information)] * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_7128.html Kasami Shrine | Tourist spots | Gifu Prefecture Tourism Official Website "Gifu Travel Guide"] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kasami Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kasami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kasami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kasami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[JA:加佐美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398477}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name=Kasami Shrine | Image=[[File:Kasami jinja buden.JPG|270px|Oratory]]
Kasami Shrine oratory (February 2011) | Address={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara City|Q641491}}{{jalink|Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109}} 5-chome 1 | location= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = Kasami Okami
{{jalink|Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} | Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} | Founded={{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}}6 years ({{jalink|864 years|Q25041}}) | Main shrine = Sangensha {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} | Annual festival={{jalink|October 14th|Q2922}}・{{jalink|October 15th|Q2919|15th}} |Refer to Shinto rituals={{jalink|#Main rituals}}| Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kasami Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kasami Shrine'' (Kasami Shrine) is {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara City|Q641491}}{{jalink|Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109}}. Located on the left bank of {{jalink|Shinsakaigawa|Q11501642}}, east-southeast of Mt. Kasami.{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shikiuchisha}}. Originally 10 villages of Sohara ({{jalink|Ibishima Village|Q18990514|Ibuki, Asuka, Shimazaki}}, {{jalink|Wago Village (Inaba District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q23940976|Noguchi, Kumada, Higashijima, Sakai}}, {{jalink|Omiya Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q18748127|Oshima/Miyashiro}}・{{jalink|Furuichi Market Village (Kakamami District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q22125133|Furuichi Market}}) is {{jalink|Soja|Q1107129}}, but it is currently considered to be the Sojajin of {{jalink|Sohara Town|Q11625179|Sohara District}} and {{jalink|Udojin|Q1392097}}. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is a Gosha. It is one of the {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} five shrines in Kakamigahara City, [https://www.tezikarao.org/news/detail.php?id=149 Kakamigahara City Kinpei Five Shrines Tour], Gifu Prefectural Shrine Agency Kakamigahara City Branch. === Summary ==={{jalink|864|Q25041}} ({{jalink|Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jokan}}6), founded by {{jalink|Emperor Seiwa|Q349243}}. In the past, this area was ruled by {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}} such as {{jalink|Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899}}, and the place name ``Sohara'' is said to have come from the Soga clan's possession. It is said that after Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro passed away, the people of Sohara Manor (currently Sohara) worshiped him and held rituals there, and the shrine itself may have existed in the {{jalink|7th century|Q8089}}. It is said that around {{jalink|905|Q24239}} ({{jalink|Enki|Q1374325}}5), it was located in {{jalink|Kakamami District|Q11412369}} Soharasho Old Market and was called Hachiman Shrine. It is said that it was called Kasami Myojin in {{jalink|908|Q30463}} (Engi 8). Furthermore, according to the middle volume of "{{jalink|Prefectural Gosha Meiji Shrine Journals|Q11486103|Fugata Gosha Meiji Shrine Journals}}" published {{jalink|1912|Q2077}} (Meiji 45), the {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} and the year of its founding are unknown{{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Gifu Prefecture |journal={{jalink|Prefecture Gosha Meiji Shrine Magazine |Q11486103|Prefectural Gosha Meiji Shrine Magazine}} |volume=Volume 2 |publisher=Meiji Shrine Magazine |date=1912-01-15 |pages=36-37 |doi=10.11501/1088278}}.In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, there is "Kasami Shrine" in the seven temples of Kagami District in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156|Minokuni}} Kaminashita |journal={{jalink|Kokushi Taikei|Q10926780|Kokushi Taikei}} |volume=13 |publisher={{jalink|Keizai Zasshisha|Q11606486}} |date=1900-11-15 |pages=346 |doi=10.11501/991103}}, "Minokuni Shinmecho" lists "{{jalink|Senior Fourth Rank|Q11123338|Senior Fourth Rank Lower}}Kasamita Myojin" among the 23 shrines in Kagami-gunza{{Cite journal |Japanese book |editor={{jalink|Yiyoshi Saeki|Q11382847}} etc. |title=Enryakuji Hon Mino Province Shinmecho |journal=Jingi Zensho |volume=Volume 4 |publisher={{jalink|Koten Kokyujo|Q11580997}} |date=1908-05-17 |pages=624 |doi=10.11501/991328}}{{Cite journal |Japanese book |editor=Kakamigahara City Board of Education |title=Section 2 Shrines as seen in the Engishiki Shinmyocho |journal=Kakamigahara City History |volume=General history Nature, primitive, ancient, medieval |publisher=Kakamigahara City |date=1986-12-20 |pages=626-630 |doi=10.11501/9571788}}. In addition, in the "Kakamigahara City History", "{{jalink|Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981}}" August 15, 864 {{jalink|Kimi|Q1062 039}}Article ``{{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}}{{jalink|Junior Fifth Rank|Q11071125|Junior Fifth Rank Lower}}Kasami Kamishōku TwoJunior Fifth Rank UpperwoOne'', but at the time of {{Efn|{{jalink|quote|Q206287}}, {{jalink|Chinese numerals|Q683566}} was changed to {{jalink|Arabic numerals|Q29961325}}. }}, the description varies depending on the manuscript. In the ``Nihon Sandai Jiroku'' published in {{jalink|1914|Q2083}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}}3), which is based on ``Mino Province'', {{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Third Dynasty Actual Record Volume 9 Emperor Seiwa (August 6th year of Teikō) |journal=Japan's Third Dynasty Actual Record |publisher=Keizai Zasshisha |date=1914-02-28 |pages=184 |quote=15th Kimimi. Mino Province Junior Fifth Rank Shimonagata no Kami. Mengami Kanda God. Kasamikami. (omitted) Parallel class2Junior Fifth Rank Upper1 {{j alink|1944|Q5268}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 19th year) "Japan Three Generations Jitsuroku" says "{{jalink|Mimasakakuni|Q936036}}"{{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Third Dynasty Actual Record Volume 9 Emperor Seiwa (August 1987) |journal=Japan's Three Dynasty Actual Record |publisher=Daiyashu Publishing Founding Office |date=1944-06-28 |pages=140 |quote=15th Kimi. Mimasakakuni Jugoi Shimonagata no Kami. Mengami Kanda God. Former shrine deity. (omitted) Parallel class 2Junior Fifth Rank Upper 1 |doi=10.11501/3456452}}. }}.In the precincts there are {{jalink|Amida-do|Q11657414}} and {{jalink|Yakushi-do|Q11622438}}, and there are remnants of {{jalink|Shinto-Buddhist syncretism|Q2279088}}. Nemotoji once existed as {{jalink|Jinguji|Q1074971}}, but in the early Meiji period, it became {{jalink|Abandoned Temple|Q11486287}} in {{jalink|Haibutsu Kishaku|Q702298}}. The main shrine (built in {{jalink|1687|Q7668}}), the shrine (built in {{jalink|1910|Q2075}}), and the worship hall (built in {{jalink|1746|Q7589}}) are |2008|Q1069941}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}20) Registered as a national {{jalink|registered tangible cultural property|Q11579194}} on April 18th {{Cite web |Japanese book |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami3.html |title=Kasami Shrine Main Hall |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami3.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{Cultural Heritage Online|144291|Kasami Shrine Main Hall}}{{Cite web |Japanese books |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami2.html |title=Kasami Shrine Heiden |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami2.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{Cultural Heritage Online|172332|Kasami Shrine Heideden}}{{Cite web |Japanese books |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami1.html |title=Kasami Shrine Hall of Worship |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami1.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{Cultural Heritage Online|186933|Kasami Shrine Worship Hall}}``Shikiuchi Kinpeisha Kasami Shrine Bookmark''.It has a deep connection with the Soga clan, and in the surrounding area there are the tomb of Soga Ishikawamaro ({{jalink|legendary Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro's tomb|Q11381424}}) and the ruins of Yamada-dera Temple ({{jalink|Yamada-dera Pagoda Heart Foundation|Q11470319}}). {{jalink|1975|Q2479}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}50th year) On September 16th, it was designated as Kinpeisha by Gifu Prefecture Shinto President Designation No. 76{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=780 |title=Kasami Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefectural Shrine Agency |accessdate=2025-05-09}}.{{jalink|Sando|Q667783}} corresponds to a part of {{jalink|Kakamigahara City#City Road Common Name|Q641491|Sohara Chuo-dori}}, and {{jalink|Otabisho|Q11489117}} is located along the approach to Sohara Furuichiba-cho 2-chome. In the past, there were two torii gates and three torii gates on the approach to the shrine, and the latter gate was connected to Sowara Chuo Dori and {{jalink|Kakamigahara City#Common names of roads in the city|Q641491|Ogase Kaido}}. The intersection, Sannotorii, is said to have existed at the intersection of Sowara Chuo-dori Street and {{jalink|Kakamigahara City#Common name for roads in the city|Q641491|Yamada-dera Street}}{{Cite book |Japanese book |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/008/305/03_kasami240815.pdf |title=Kakamigahara City District Landscape Plan Landscape Formation Standards Guidelines Key Scenic District 3 Kasami Shrine District |format=PDF |publisher=Kakamigahara City |page=1 |accessdate=2025-02-26}}.Kasami Shrine once had Ichinomiya, Ninomiya, and Sannomiya, and Ichinomiya was the Shinmei Shrine in Furuichiba Village, Kagami District (now Sohara Furuichiba Town). (Kakamigahara City, Sohara Furuichiba Town)|Q134987025|Shinmei Shrine}}, Ninomiya is {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine} in Ibuki Village, Kakamiga District (currently {{jalink|Sohara Ibuki Town|Q131900799}}) (Sohara Ibuki-cho, Kakamigahara City)|Q134928112|Yasaka Shrine}}, Sannomiya is Kumano Shrine {{Efn|{{jalink|1909|Q2057}} (Meiji 42), {{jalink|Shinmei Shrine (Sohara Noguchi Town, Kakamigahara City)|Q134926762|Shinmei Shrine}} ({{jalink|Sohara Noguchi Town|Q131870111}}). }}From the local commentary board ``Kasami Shrine "Nino Torii" base stone''. Based on the Kakamigahara City {{jalink|Landscape Ordinance|Q11514689|Urban Landscape Ordinance}}, on August 1, 2008|Q2004, Kakamigahara City Notification No. 80 designated approximately 37.29{{jalink|ha|Q35852}} surrounding the shrine as a key scenic area.{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/shisei/shisaku/1008207/1008297/1008302/1008305.html |title=Important scenic areas (determined) Districts with historical resources and scenery |publisher=Kakamigahara City |date=2024-08-16 |accessdate=2025-05-09}}.During the {{jalink|Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|Sengoku period}}, Kasami Shrine was called {{jalink|Hachimangu|Q11390939}}, and Mt. Kasami on the mountain behind the shrine was also called Mt. Hachiman, and {{jalink|Hachimanyama Castle (Mino Province)|Q38276751|Hachimanyama Castle}} === Enshrined God === * Kasami Okami ** According to one theory, ``Kasamiyama'' itself, located to the west-northwest of Kasami Shrine, was once an object of worship. It is assumed that Kasami Okami considered Mt. Kasami as a god. * {{jalink|Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899}} * {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} === Precinct Company === * Akiba Shrine ({{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Katsuchijin}}) * Okuwa Shrine ({{jalink|Toyoukebime|Q2983724|Toyouke Okami}}) * Kasami Inari Shrine ({{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Kuraina Tama no Mikoto}}) * Shinmei Shrine ({{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602}}) === Others === * {{jalink|Koshin Tower|Q11486001}} ** Built in {{jalink|1980|Q2439}} (1980) using one of the cornerstones of the former second torii gate of Kasami Shrine. * {{jalink|Loyalty Monument|Q11274518}} ({{jalink|Sino-Japanese War|Q178687|Conquering and Qing Monument}}, {{jalink|Russo-Japanese War|Q159950|Russo-Japanese War}} Loyalty Monument, Martyred Soldiers Monument) ** It was originally installed in a different location, but was moved to the shrine grounds. === Main rituals === Based on "Shikiuchi Kinpeisha Kasami Shrine Bookmark". * January 1st - {{jalink|Saidan Festival|Q11546824}}, Good Luck Prayer Festival, Traffic Safety Festival * February Setsubun - {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798}} Festival * February 14th of the lunar calendar - Akiba Festival * February 20th - {{jalink|prayer festival|Q118906710}}・Okuwa Festival * March 15th - Sumiyoshi Festival * First Saturday of April - Okunomiya Festival/Suijin Festival * Last Sunday of July - {{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910}} Shinto ritual * Second Sunday before October 15th - {{jalink|Reitaisai|Q11385469|Reitaisai}} ** It used to be held on October 14th and 15th every year. A flower mikoshi is carried out. Previously, the Kenka Mikoshi was dedicated, but it was temporarily suspended due to an accident, but the "Rokken Mikoshi Association" centered around {{jalink|Sohara Rokkencho|Q131900816}} has revived the Kenka Mikoshi as a mock performance.{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://rokken-mikoshi.jimdosite.com/ |title=Rokuken Mikoshi Association |accessdate=2025-05-09}}{{Cite news |Japanese book |url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/791540 |title=Vertically ``Fighting Mikoshi'' Regular festival at Kakamigahara Kasami Shrine |newspaper={{jalink|Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678}} |date=2023-10-19}}.* November 15th - {{jalink|Shichigosan|Q1072387}} Festival * November 23rd - New Grain Thanksgiving * December 31st - {{jalink|New Year's Eve|Q7245702}} Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Monthly Festival === Cultural assets === * Wooden guardian dog ** A wooden {{jalink|guardian dog|Q20155846}} approximately 115cm tall. It is thought to have been produced in the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} period. City-designated cultural property ({{jalink|1974|Q2478}} (June 26, 1974)) {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-designated cultural property |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005217.html |title=Is there actually only one “guardian dog”? Elegant wooden sculpture |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}. * Kasami Shrine Lion Head** A wooden lion head donated by Noguchi Village, Kagami District in {{jalink|Enpo|Q1145735}}3 ({{jalink|1675|Q7624}}). During the annual shrine festival procession in October, a lion head leads the mikoshi procession. City-designated cultural property ({{jalink|1970|Q2474}} (March 23, 1974)) {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005225.html |title=A regional treasure where the legend of "Oshishi-sama" is passed down |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}. === Location === * {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara City|Q641491}}{{jalink|Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109}} 5-chome 1 === Transportation === * {{jalink|Gifu Shared Car|Q5560101|Gifu Bus}} Get off at Ozaki Danchi Line "Sohara" {{jalink|Bus Stop|Q953806|Bus Stop}} and walk for about 10 minutes. ** {{jalink|JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal|Q11226229}} Platform 13 "Suwayama Danchi" "{{jalink|Gifu Prefectural Kakamigahara High School|Q11471490|Kakamigahara High School}}" "{{jalink|Techno Plaza (Gifu Prefecture)|Q11319203|Techno Plaza}}" and "{{jalink|Mikakino Station|Q6845663}}". ** {{jalink|Meitetsu Gifu Bus Stop|Q11415771}} Take the bus bound for ``Suwayama Danchi,'' ``Kakamigahara High School,'' ``Techno Plaza,'' and ``Mikakino Station'' at bus stop 6. * {{jalink|Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316}} ** Get off at Sohara Line "Sohara Furuichibacho" bus stop and walk for about 8 minutes. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Terikio Shrine (Kakamigahara City)|Q11496922}} * {{jalink|Murakuni Shrine|Q11523055}} * {{jalink|Murakuni Masumida Shrine|Q11523053}} * {{jalink|Mii Shrine (Kakamigahara City)|Q11488771}} * {{jalink|List of registered tangible cultural properties in Gifu Prefecture|Q119930208}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Kasami-jinja}} * {{Instagram|kasami_jinjya_|Kasami Shrine}} * {{kotobank}} * [https://kakamigahara-kankou.jp/tourism/256 Kasami Shrine｜Kakamigahara Walk (Kakamigahara City Tourist Information)] * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_7128.html
