[[JA:加佐美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398477}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name=Kasami Shrine | Image=[[File:Kasami jinja buden.JPG|270px|Oratory]]
Kasami Shrine oratory (February 2011) | Address={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara City|Q641491}}{{jalink|Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109}} 5-chome 1 | location= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = Kasami Okami
{{jalink|Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} | Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} | Founded={{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}}6 years ({{jalink|864 years|Q25041}}) | Main shrine = Sangensha {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} | Annual festival={{jalink|October 14th|Q2922}}・{{jalink|October 15th|Q2919|15th}} |Refer to Shinto rituals={{jalink|#Main rituals}}| Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kasami Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kasami Shrine'' (Kasami Shrine) is {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara City|Q641491}}{{jalink|Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109}}. Located on the left bank of {{jalink|Shinsakaigawa|Q11501642}}, east-southeast of Mt. Kasami.{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shikiuchisha}}. Originally 10 villages of Sohara ({{jalink|Ibishima Village|Q18990514|Ibuki, Asuka, Shimazaki}}, {{jalink|Wago Village (Inaba District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q23940976|Noguchi, Kumada, Higashijima, Sakai}}, {{jalink|Omiya Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q18748127|Oshima/Miyashiro}}・{{jalink|Furuichi Market Village (Kakamami District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q22125133|Furuichi Market}}) is {{jalink|Soja|Q1107129}}, but it is currently considered to be the Sojajin of {{jalink|Sohara Town|Q11625179|Sohara District}} and {{jalink|Udojin|Q1392097}}. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is a Gosha. It is one of the {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} five shrines in Kakamigahara City, [https://www.tezikarao.org/news/detail.php?id=149 Kakamigahara City Kinpei Five Shrines Tour], Gifu Prefectural Shrine Agency Kakamigahara City Branch. === Summary ==={{jalink|864|Q25041}} ({{jalink|Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jokan}}6), founded by {{jalink|Emperor Seiwa|Q349243}}. In the past, this area was ruled by {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}} such as {{jalink|Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899}}, and the place name ``Sohara'' is said to have come from the Soga clan's possession. It is said that after Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro passed away, the people of Sohara Manor (currently Sohara) worshiped him and held rituals there, and the shrine itself may have existed in the {{jalink|7th century|Q8089}}. It is said that around {{jalink|905|Q24239}} ({{jalink|Enki|Q1374325}}5), it was located in {{jalink|Kakamami District|Q11412369}} Soharasho Old Market and was called Hachiman Shrine. It is said that it was called Kasami Myojin in {{jalink|908|Q30463}} (Engi 8). Furthermore, according to the middle volume of "{{jalink|Prefectural Gosha Meiji Shrine Journals|Q11486103|Fugata Gosha Meiji Shrine Journals}}" published {{jalink|1912|Q2077}} (Meiji 45), the {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} and the year of its founding are unknown{{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Gifu Prefecture |journal={{jalink|Prefecture Gosha Meiji Shrine Magazine |Q11486103|Prefectural Gosha Meiji Shrine Magazine}} |volume=Volume 2 |publisher=Meiji Shrine Magazine |date=1912-01-15 |pages=36-37 |doi=10.11501/1088278}}.In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, there is "Kasami Shrine" in the seven temples of Kagami District in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156|Minokuni}} Kaminashita |journal={{jalink|Kokushi Taikei|Q10926780|Kokushi Taikei}} |volume=13 |publisher={{jalink|Keizai Zasshisha|Q11606486}} |date=1900-11-15 |pages=346 |doi=10.11501/991103}}, "Minokuni Shinmecho" lists "{{jalink|Senior Fourth Rank|Q11123338|Senior Fourth Rank Lower}}Kasamita Myojin" among the 23 shrines in Kagami-gunza{{Cite journal |Japanese book |editor={{jalink|Yiyoshi Saeki|Q11382847}} etc. |title=Enryakuji Hon Mino Province Shinmecho |journal=Jingi Zensho |volume=Volume 4 |publisher={{jalink|Koten Kokyujo|Q11580997}} |date=1908-05-17 |pages=624 |doi=10.11501/991328}}{{Cite journal |Japanese book |editor=Kakamigahara City Board of Education |title=Section 2 Shrines as seen in the Engishiki Shinmyocho |journal=Kakamigahara City History |volume=General history Nature, primitive, ancient, medieval |publisher=Kakamigahara City |date=1986-12-20 |pages=626-630 |doi=10.11501/9571788}}. In addition, in the "Kakamigahara City History", "{{jalink|Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981}}" August 15, 864 {{jalink|Kimi|Q1062 039}}Article ``{{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}}{{jalink|Junior Fifth Rank|Q11071125|Junior Fifth Rank Lower}}Kasami Kamishōku TwoJunior Fifth Rank UpperwoOne'', but at the time of {{Efn|{{jalink|quote|Q206287}}, {{jalink|Chinese numerals|Q683566}} was changed to {{jalink|Arabic numerals|Q29961325}}. }}, the description varies depending on the manuscript. In the ``Nihon Sandai Jiroku'' published in {{jalink|1914|Q2083}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}}3), which is based on ``Mino Province'', {{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Third Dynasty Actual Record Volume 9 Emperor Seiwa (August 6th year of Teikō) |journal=Japan's Third Dynasty Actual Record |publisher=Keizai Zasshisha |date=1914-02-28 |pages=184 |quote=15th Kimimi. Mino Province Junior Fifth Rank Shimonagata no Kami. Mengami Kanda God. Kasamikami. (omitted) Parallel class2Junior Fifth Rank Upper1 {{j alink|1944|Q5268}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 19th year) "Japan Three Generations Jitsuroku" says "{{jalink|Mimasakakuni|Q936036}}"{{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Third Dynasty Actual Record Volume 9 Emperor Seiwa (August 1987) |journal=Japan's Three Dynasty Actual Record |publisher=Daiyashu Publishing Founding Office |date=1944-06-28 |pages=140 |quote=15th Kimi. Mimasakakuni Jugoi Shimonagata no Kami. Mengami Kanda God. Former shrine deity. (omitted) Parallel class 2Junior Fifth Rank Upper 1 |doi=10.11501/3456452}}. }}.In the precincts there are {{jalink|Amida-do|Q11657414}} and {{jalink|Yakushi-do|Q11622438}}, and there are remnants of {{jalink|Shinto-Buddhist syncretism|Q2279088}}. Nemotoji once existed as {{jalink|Jinguji|Q1074971}}, but in the early Meiji period, it became {{jalink|Abandoned Temple|Q11486287}} in {{jalink|Haibutsu Kishaku|Q702298}}. The main shrine (built in {{jalink|1687|Q7668}}), the shrine (built in {{jalink|1910|Q2075}}), and the worship hall (built in {{jalink|1746|Q7589}}) are |2008|Q1069941}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}20) Registered as a national {{jalink|registered tangible cultural property|Q11579194}} on April 18th {{Cite web |Japanese book |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{Cultural Heritage Online|144291|Kasami Shrine Main Hall}}{{Cite web |Japanese books |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami2.html |title=Kasami Shrine Heiden |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami1.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{Cultural Heritage Online|186933|Kasami Shrine Worship Hall}}``Shikiuchi Kinpeisha Kasami Shrine Bookmark''.It has a deep connection with the Soga clan, and in the surrounding area there are the tomb of Soga Ishikawamaro ({{jalink|legendary Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro's tomb|Q11381424}}) and the ruins of Yamada-dera Temple ({{jalink|Yamada-dera Pagoda Heart Foundation|Q11470319}}). {{jalink|1975|Q2479}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}50th year) On September 16th, it was designated as Kinpeisha by Gifu Prefecture Shinto President Designation No. 76{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=780 |title=Kasami Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefectural Shrine Agency |accessdate=2025-05-09}}.{{jalink|Sando|Q667783}} corresponds to a part of {{jalink|Kakamigahara City#City Road Common Name|Q641491|Sohara Chuo-dori}}, and {{jalink|Otabisho|Q11489117}} is located along the approach to Sohara Furuichiba-cho 2-chome. In the past, there were two torii gates and three torii gates on the approach to the shrine, and the latter gate was connected to Sowara Chuo Dori and {{jalink|Kakamigahara City#Common names of roads in the city|Q641491|Ogase Kaido}}. The intersection, Sannotorii, is said to have existed at the intersection of Sowara Chuo-dori Street and {{jalink|Kakamigahara City#Common name for roads in the city|Q641491|Yamada-dera Street}}{{Cite book |Japanese book |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/008/305/03_kasami240815.pdf |title=Kakamigahara City District Landscape Plan Landscape Formation Standards Guidelines Key Scenic District 3 Kasami Shrine District |format=PDF |publisher=Kakamigahara City |page=1 |accessdate=2025-02-26}}.Kasami Shrine once had Ichinomiya, Ninomiya, and Sannomiya, and Ichinomiya was the Shinmei Shrine in Furuichiba Village, Kagami District (now Sohara Furuichiba Town). (Kakamigahara City, Sohara Furuichiba Town)|Q134987025|Shinmei Shrine}}, Ninomiya is {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine} in Ibuki Village, Kakamiga District (currently {{jalink|Sohara Ibuki Town|Q131900799}}) (Sohara Ibuki-cho, Kakamigahara City)|Q134928112|Yasaka Shrine}}, Sannomiya is Kumano Shrine {{Efn|{{jalink|1909|Q2057}} (Meiji 42), {{jalink|Shinmei Shrine (Sohara Noguchi Town, Kakamigahara City)|Q134926762|Shinmei Shrine}} ({{jalink|Sohara Noguchi Town|Q131870111}}). }}From the local commentary board ``Kasami Shrine "Nino Torii" base stone''. Based on the Kakamigahara City {{jalink|Landscape Ordinance|Q11514689|Urban Landscape Ordinance}}, on August 1, 2008|Q2004, Kakamigahara City Notification No. 80 designated approximately 37.29{{jalink|ha|Q35852}} surrounding the shrine as a key scenic area.{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/shisei/shisaku/1008207/1008297/1008302/1008305.html |title=Important scenic areas (determined) Districts with historical resources and scenery |publisher=Kakamigahara City |date=2024-08-16 |accessdate=2025-05-09}}.During the {{jalink|Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|Sengoku period}}, Kasami Shrine was called {{jalink|Hachimangu|Q11390939}}, and Mt. Kasami on the mountain behind the shrine was also called Mt. Hachiman, and {{jalink|Hachimanyama Castle (Mino Province)|Q38276751|Hachimanyama Castle}} === Enshrined God === * Kasami Okami ** According to one theory, ``Kasamiyama'' itself, located to the west-northwest of Kasami Shrine, was once an object of worship. It is assumed that Kasami Okami considered Mt. Kasami as a god. * {{jalink|Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899}} * {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} === Precinct Company === * Akiba Shrine ({{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Katsuchijin}}) * Okuwa Shrine ({{jalink|Toyoukebime|Q2983724|Toyouke Okami}}) * Kasami Inari Shrine ({{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Kuraina Tama no Mikoto}}) * Shinmei Shrine ({{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602}}) === Others === * {{jalink|Koshin Tower|Q11486001}} ** Built in {{jalink|1980|Q2439}} (1980) using one of the cornerstones of the former second torii gate of Kasami Shrine. * {{jalink|Loyalty Monument|Q11274518}} ({{jalink|Sino-Japanese War|Q178687|Conquering and Qing Monument}}, {{jalink|Russo-Japanese War|Q159950|Russo-Japanese War}} Loyalty Monument, Martyred Soldiers Monument) ** It was originally installed in a different location, but was moved to the shrine grounds. === Main rituals === Based on "Shikiuchi Kinpeisha Kasami Shrine Bookmark". * January 1st - {{jalink|Saidan Festival|Q11546824}}, Good Luck Prayer Festival, Traffic Safety Festival * February Setsubun - {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798}} Festival * February 14th of the lunar calendar - Akiba Festival * February 20th - {{jalink|prayer festival|Q118906710}}・Okuwa Festival * March 15th - Sumiyoshi Festival * First Saturday of April - Okunomiya Festival/Suijin Festival * Last Sunday of July - {{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910}} Shinto ritual * Second Sunday before October 15th - {{jalink|Reitaisai|Q11385469|Reitaisai}} ** It used to be held on October 14th and 15th every year. A flower mikoshi is carried out. Previously, the Kenka Mikoshi was dedicated, but it was temporarily suspended due to an accident, but the "Rokken Mikoshi Association" centered around {{jalink|Sohara Rokkencho|Q131900816}} has revived the Kenka Mikoshi as a mock performance.{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://rokken-mikoshi.jimdosite.com/ |title=Rokuken Mikoshi Association |accessdate=2025-05-09}}{{Cite news |Japanese book |url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/791540 |title=Vertically ``Fighting Mikoshi'' Regular festival at Kakamigahara Kasami Shrine |newspaper={{jalink|Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678}} |date=2023-10-19}}.* November 15th - {{jalink|Shichigosan|Q1072387}} Festival * November 23rd - New Grain Thanksgiving * December 31st - {{jalink|New Year's Eve|Q7245702}} Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Monthly Festival === Cultural assets === * Wooden guardian dog ** A wooden {{jalink|guardian dog|Q20155846}} approximately 115cm tall. It is thought to have been produced in the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} period. City-designated cultural property ({{jalink|1974|Q2478}} (June 26, 1974)) {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-designated cultural property |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005217.html |title=Is there actually only one “guardian dog”? Elegant wooden sculpture |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}. * Kasami Shrine Lion Head** A wooden lion head donated by Noguchi Village, Kagami District in {{jalink|Enpo|Q1145735}}3 ({{jalink|1675|Q7624}}). During the annual shrine festival procession in October, a lion head leads the mikoshi procession. City-designated cultural property ({{jalink|1970|Q2474}} (March 23, 1974)) {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005225.html |title=A regional treasure where the legend of "Oshishi-sama" is passed down |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}. === Location === * {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara City|Q641491}}{{jalink|Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109}} 5-chome 1 === Transportation === * {{jalink|Gifu Shared Car|Q5560101|Gifu Bus}} Get off at Ozaki Danchi Line "Sohara" {{jalink|Bus Stop|Q953806|Bus Stop}} and walk for about 10 minutes. ** {{jalink|JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal|Q11226229}} Platform 13 "Suwayama Danchi" "{{jalink|Gifu Prefectural Kakamigahara High School|Q11471490|Kakamigahara High School}}" "{{jalink|Techno Plaza (Gifu Prefecture)|Q11319203|Techno Plaza}}" and "{{jalink|Mikakino Station|Q6845663}}". ** {{jalink|Meitetsu Gifu Bus Stop|Q11415771}} Take the bus bound for ``Suwayama Danchi,'' ``Kakamigahara High School,'' ``Techno Plaza,'' and ``Mikakino Station'' at bus stop 6. * {{jalink|Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316}} ** Get off at Sohara Line "Sohara Furuichibacho" bus stop and walk for about 8 minutes. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items [[JA:加佐美神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11398477}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name=Kasami Shrine | Image=[[File:Kasami jinja buden.JPG|270px|Oratory]]
Kasami Shrine oratory (February 2011) | Address={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara City|Q641491}}{{jalink|Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109}} 5-chome 1 | location= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = Kasami Okami
{{jalink|Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} | Company rank={{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}
{{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} | Founded={{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}}6 years ({{jalink|864 years|Q25041}}) | Main shrine = Sangensha {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} | Annual festival={{jalink|October 14th|Q2922}}・{{jalink|October 15th|Q2919|15th}} |Refer to Shinto rituals={{jalink|#Main rituals}}| Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kasami Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kasami Shrine'' (Kasami Shrine) is {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara City|Q641491}}{{jalink|Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109}}. Located on the left bank of {{jalink|Shinsakaigawa|Q11501642}}, east-southeast of Mt. Kasami.{{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shikiuchisha}}. Originally 10 villages of Sohara ({{jalink|Ibishima Village|Q18990514|Ibuki, Asuka, Shimazaki}}, {{jalink|Wago Village (Inaba District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q23940976|Noguchi, Kumada, Higashijima, Sakai}}, {{jalink|Omiya Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q18748127|Oshima/Miyashiro}}・{{jalink|Furuichi Market Village (Kakamami District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q22125133|Furuichi Market}}) is {{jalink|Soja|Q1107129}}, but it is currently considered to be the Sojajin of {{jalink|Sohara Town|Q11625179|Sohara District}} and {{jalink|Udojin|Q1392097}}. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is a Gosha. It is one of the {{jalink|Kinpeisha|Q119929592}} five shrines in Kakamigahara City, [https://www.tezikarao.org/news/detail.php?id=149 Kakamigahara City Kinpei Five Shrines Tour], Gifu Prefectural Shrine Agency Kakamigahara City Branch. === Summary ==={{jalink|864|Q25041}} ({{jalink|Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jokan}}6), founded by {{jalink|Emperor Seiwa|Q349243}}. In the past, this area was ruled by {{jalink|Soga clan|Q909854}} such as {{jalink|Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899}}, and the place name ``Sohara'' is said to have come from the Soga clan's possession. It is said that after Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro passed away, the people of Sohara Manor (currently Sohara) worshiped him and held rituals there, and the shrine itself may have existed in the {{jalink|7th century|Q8089}}. It is said that around {{jalink|905|Q24239}} ({{jalink|Enki|Q1374325}}5), it was located in {{jalink|Kakamami District|Q11412369}} Soharasho Old Market and was called Hachiman Shrine. It is said that it was called Kasami Myojin in {{jalink|908|Q30463}} (Engi 8). Furthermore, according to the middle volume of "{{jalink|Prefectural Gosha Meiji Shrine Journals|Q11486103|Fugata Gosha Meiji Shrine Journals}}" published {{jalink|1912|Q2077}} (Meiji 45), the {{jalink|enshrined deity|Q11591100}} and the year of its founding are unknown{{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Gifu Prefecture |journal={{jalink|Prefecture Gosha Meiji Shrine Magazine |Q11486103|Prefectural Gosha Meiji Shrine Magazine}} |volume=Volume 2 |publisher=Meiji Shrine Magazine |date=1912-01-15 |pages=36-37 |doi=10.11501/1088278}}.In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, there is "Kasami Shrine" in the seven temples of Kagami District in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156|Minokuni}} Kaminashita |journal={{jalink|Kokushi Taikei|Q10926780|Kokushi Taikei}} |volume=13 |publisher={{jalink|Keizai Zasshisha|Q11606486}} |date=1900-11-15 |pages=346 |doi=10.11501/991103}}, "Minokuni Shinmecho" lists "{{jalink|Senior Fourth Rank|Q11123338|Senior Fourth Rank Lower}}Kasamita Myojin" among the 23 shrines in Kagami-gunza{{Cite journal |Japanese book |editor={{jalink|Yiyoshi Saeki|Q11382847}} etc. |title=Enryakuji Hon Mino Province Shinmecho |journal=Jingi Zensho |volume=Volume 4 |publisher={{jalink|Koten Kokyujo|Q11580997}} |date=1908-05-17 |pages=624 |doi=10.11501/991328}}{{Cite journal |Japanese book |editor=Kakamigahara City Board of Education |title=Section 2 Shrines as seen in the Engishiki Shinmyocho |journal=Kakamigahara City History |volume=General history Nature, primitive, ancient, medieval |publisher=Kakamigahara City |date=1986-12-20 |pages=626-630 |doi=10.11501/9571788}}. In addition, in the "Kakamigahara City History", "{{jalink|Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981}}" August 15, 864 {{jalink|Kimi|Q1062 039}}Article ``{{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}}{{jalink|Junior Fifth Rank|Q11071125|Junior Fifth Rank Lower}}Kasami Kamishōku TwoJunior Fifth Rank UpperwoOne'', but at the time of {{Efn|{{jalink|quote|Q206287}}, {{jalink|Chinese numerals|Q683566}} was changed to {{jalink|Arabic numerals|Q29961325}}. }}, the description varies depending on the manuscript. In the ``Nihon Sandai Jiroku'' published in {{jalink|1914|Q2083}} ({{jalink|Taisho|Q329174}}3), which is based on ``Mino Province'', {{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Third Dynasty Actual Record Volume 9 Emperor Seiwa (August 6th year of Teikō) |journal=Japan's Third Dynasty Actual Record |publisher=Keizai Zasshisha |date=1914-02-28 |pages=184 |quote=15th Kimimi. Mino Province Junior Fifth Rank Shimonagata no Kami. Mengami Kanda God. Kasamikami. (omitted) Parallel class2Junior Fifth Rank Upper1 {{j alink|1944|Q5268}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 19th year) "Japan Three Generations Jitsuroku" says "{{jalink|Mimasakakuni|Q936036}}"{{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Third Dynasty Actual Record Volume 9 Emperor Seiwa (August 1987) |journal=Japan's Three Dynasty Actual Record |publisher=Daiyashu Publishing Founding Office |date=1944-06-28 |pages=140 |quote=15th Kimi. Mimasakakuni Jugoi Shimonagata no Kami. Mengami Kanda God. Former shrine deity. (omitted) Parallel class 2Junior Fifth Rank Upper 1 |doi=10.11501/3456452}}. }}.In the precincts there are {{jalink|Amida-do|Q11657414}} and {{jalink|Yakushi-do|Q11622438}}, and there are remnants of {{jalink|Shinto-Buddhist syncretism|Q2279088}}. Nemotoji once existed as {{jalink|Jinguji|Q1074971}}, but in the early Meiji period, it became {{jalink|Abandoned Temple|Q11486287}} in {{jalink|Haibutsu Kishaku|Q702298}}. The main shrine (built in {{jalink|1687|Q7668}}), the shrine (built in {{jalink|1910|Q2075}}), and the worship hall (built in {{jalink|1746|Q7589}}) are |2008|Q1069941}} ({{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}20) Registered as a national {{jalink|registered tangible cultural property|Q11579194}} on April 18th {{Cite web |Japanese book |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami3.html |title=Kasami Shrine Main Hall |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami3.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{Cultural Heritage Online|144291|Kasami Shrine Main Hall}}{{Cite web |Japanese books |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami2.html |title=Kasami Shrine Heiden |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami2.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{Cultural Heritage Online|172332|Kasami Shrine Heideden}}{{Cite web |Japanese books |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami1.html |title=Kasami Shrine Hall of Worship |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami1.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{Cultural Heritage Online|186933|Kasami Shrine Worship Hall}}``Shikiuchi Kinpeisha Kasami Shrine Bookmark''.It has a deep connection with the Soga clan, and in the surrounding area there are the tomb of Soga Ishikawamaro ({{jalink|legendary Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro's tomb|Q11381424}}) and the ruins of Yamada-dera Temple ({{jalink|Yamada-dera Pagoda Heart Foundation|Q11470319}}). {{jalink|1975|Q2479}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}50th year) On September 16th, it was designated as Kinpeisha by Gifu Prefecture Shinto President Designation No. 76{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=780 |title=Kasami Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefectural Shrine Agency |accessdate=2025-05-09}}.{{jalink|Sando|Q667783}} corresponds to a part of {{jalink|Kakamigahara City#City Road Common Name|Q641491|Sohara Chuo-dori}}, and {{jalink|Otabisho|Q11489117}} is located along the approach to Sohara Furuichiba-cho 2-chome. In the past, there were two torii gates and three torii gates on the approach to the shrine, and the latter gate was connected to Sowara Chuo Dori and {{jalink|Kakamigahara City#Common names of roads in the city|Q641491|Ogase Kaido}}. The intersection, Sannotorii, is said to have existed at the intersection of Sowara Chuo-dori Street and {{jalink|Kakamigahara City#Common name for roads in the city|Q641491|Yamada-dera Street}}{{Cite book |Japanese book |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/008/305/03_kasami240815.pdf |title=Kakamigahara City District Landscape Plan Landscape Formation Standards Guidelines Key Scenic District 3 Kasami Shrine District |format=PDF |publisher=Kakamigahara City |page=1 |accessdate=2025-02-26}}.Kasami Shrine once had Ichinomiya, Ninomiya, and Sannomiya, and Ichinomiya was the Shinmei Shrine in Furuichiba Village, Kagami District (now Sohara Furuichiba Town). (Kakamigahara City, Sohara Furuichiba Town)|Q134987025|Shinmei Shrine}}, Ninomiya is {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine} in Ibuki Village, Kakamiga District (currently {{jalink|Sohara Ibuki Town|Q131900799}}) (Sohara Ibuki-cho, Kakamigahara City)|Q134928112|Yasaka Shrine}}, Sannomiya is Kumano Shrine {{Efn|{{jalink|1909|Q2057}} (Meiji 42), {{jalink|Shinmei Shrine (Sohara Noguchi Town, Kakamigahara City)|Q134926762|Shinmei Shrine}} ({{jalink|Sohara Noguchi Town|Q131870111}}). }}From the local commentary board ``Kasami Shrine "Nino Torii" base stone''. Based on the Kakamigahara City {{jalink|Landscape Ordinance|Q11514689|Urban Landscape Ordinance}}, on August 1, 2008|Q2004, Kakamigahara City Notification No. 80 designated approximately 37.29{{jalink|ha|Q35852}} surrounding the shrine as a key scenic area.{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/shisei/shisaku/1008207/1008297/1008302/1008305.html |title=Important scenic areas (determined) Districts with historical resources and scenery |publisher=Kakamigahara City |date=2024-08-16 |accessdate=2025-05-09}}.During the {{jalink|Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|Sengoku period}}, Kasami Shrine was called {{jalink|Hachimangu|Q11390939}}, and Mt. Kasami on the mountain behind the shrine was also called Mt. Hachiman, and {{jalink|Hachimanyama Castle (Mino Province)|Q38276751|Hachimanyama Castle}} === Enshrined God === * Kasami Okami ** According to one theory, ``Kasamiyama'' itself, located to the west-northwest of Kasami Shrine, was once an object of worship. It is assumed that Kasami Okami considered Mt. Kasami as a god. * {{jalink|Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899}} * {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} === Precinct Company === * Akiba Shrine ({{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Katsuchijin}}) * Okuwa Shrine ({{jalink|Toyoukebime|Q2983724|Toyouke Okami}}) * Kasami Inari Shrine ({{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Kuraina Tama no Mikoto}}) * Shinmei Shrine ({{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602}}) === Others === * {{jalink|Koshin Tower|Q11486001}} ** Built in {{jalink|1980|Q2439}} (1980) using one of the cornerstones of the former second torii gate of Kasami Shrine. * {{jalink|Loyalty Monument|Q11274518}} ({{jalink|Sino-Japanese War|Q178687|Conquering and Qing Monument}}, {{jalink|Russo-Japanese War|Q159950|Russo-Japanese War}} Loyalty Monument, Martyred Soldiers Monument) ** It was originally installed in a different location, but was moved to the shrine grounds. === Main rituals === Based on "Shikiuchi Kinpeisha Kasami Shrine Bookmark". * January 1st - {{jalink|Saidan Festival|Q11546824}}, Good Luck Prayer Festival, Traffic Safety Festival * February Setsubun - {{jalink|Setsubun|Q746798}} Festival * February 14th of the lunar calendar - Akiba Festival * February 20th - {{jalink|prayer festival|Q118906710}}・Okuwa Festival * March 15th - Sumiyoshi Festival * First Saturday of April - Okunomiya Festival/Suijin Festival * Last Sunday of July - {{jalink|Great Purification|Q11261910}} Shinto ritual * Second Sunday before October 15th - {{jalink|Reitaisai|Q11385469|Reitaisai}} ** It used to be held on October 14th and 15th every year. A flower mikoshi is carried out. Previously, the Kenka Mikoshi was dedicated, but it was temporarily suspended due to an accident, but the "Rokken Mikoshi Association" centered around {{jalink|Sohara Rokkencho|Q131900816}} has revived the Kenka Mikoshi as a mock performance.{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://rokken-mikoshi.jimdosite.com/ |title=Rokuken Mikoshi Association |accessdate=2025-05-09}}{{Cite news |Japanese book |url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/791540 |title=Vertically ``Fighting Mikoshi'' Regular festival at Kakamigahara Kasami Shrine |newspaper={{jalink|Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678}} |date=2023-10-19}}.* November 15th - {{jalink|Shichigosan|Q1072387}} Festival * November 23rd - New Grain Thanksgiving * December 31st - {{jalink|New Year's Eve|Q7245702}} Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Monthly Festival === Cultural assets === * Wooden guardian dog ** A wooden {{jalink|guardian dog|Q20155846}} approximately 115cm tall. It is thought to have been produced in the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} period. City-designated cultural property ({{jalink|1974|Q2478}} (June 26, 1974)) {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-designated cultural property |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005217.html |title=Is there actually only one “guardian dog”? Elegant wooden sculpture |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}. * Kasami Shrine Lion Head** A wooden lion head donated by Noguchi Village, Kagami District in {{jalink|Enpo|Q1145735}}3 ({{jalink|1675|Q7624}}). During the annual shrine festival procession in October, a lion head leads the mikoshi procession. City-designated cultural property ({{jalink|1970|Q2474}} (March 23, 1974)) {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005225.html |title=A regional treasure where the legend of "Oshishi-sama" is passed down |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}. === Location === * {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kakamigahara City|Q641491}}{{jalink|Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109}} 5-chome 1 === Transportation === * {{jalink|Gifu Shared Car|Q5560101|Gifu Bus}} Get off at Ozaki Danchi Line "Sohara" {{jalink|Bus Stop|Q953806|Bus Stop}} and walk for about 10 minutes. ** {{jalink|JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal|Q11226229}} Platform 13 "Suwayama Danchi" "{{jalink|Gifu Prefectural Kakamigahara High School|Q11471490|Kakamigahara High School}}" "{{jalink|Techno Plaza (Gifu Prefecture)|Q11319203|Techno Plaza}}" and "{{jalink|Mikakino Station|Q6845663}}". ** {{jalink|Meitetsu Gifu Bus Stop|Q11415771}} Take the bus bound for ``Suwayama Danchi,'' ``Kakamigahara High School,'' ``Techno Plaza,'' and ``Mikakino Station'' at bus stop 6. * {{jalink|Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316}} Kasami Shrine oratory (February 2011) | Address={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491}}{{ill|lt=Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109|WD=Q131870109}} 5-chome 1 | location= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = Kasami Okami
{{ill|lt=Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} | Company rank={{ill||lt=Shikiuchisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}
{{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} | Founded={{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}}6 years ({{ill|lt=864 years|Q25041|WD=Q25041}}) | Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 14th|Q2922|WD=Q2922}}・{{ill|lt=October 15th|Q2919|WD=Q2919|lt=15th}} |Refer to Shinto rituals={{ill||lt=#Main rituals|UNKNOWN}}| Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kasami Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kasami Shrine'' (Kasami Shrine) is {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491}}{{ill|lt=Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109|WD=Q131870109}}. Located on the left bank of {{ill|lt=Shinsakaigawa|Q11501642|WD=Q11501642}}, east-southeast of Mt. Kasami.{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikiuchisha}}. Originally 10 villages of Sohara ({{ill|lt=Ibishima Village|Q18990514|WD=Q18990514|lt=Ibuki, Asuka, Shimazaki}}, {{ill|lt=Wago Village (Inaba District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q23940976|WD=Q23940976|lt=Noguchi, Kumada, Higashijima, Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Omiya Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q18748127|WD=Q18748127|lt=Oshima/Miyashiro}}・{{ill|lt=Furuichi Market Village (Kakamami District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q22125133|WD=Q22125133|lt=Furuichi Market}}) is {{ill|lt=Soja|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129}}, but it is currently considered to be the Sojajin of {{ill|lt=Sohara Town|Q11625179|WD=Q11625179|lt=Sohara District}} and {{ill|lt=Udojin|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097}}. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is a Gosha. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} five shrines in Kakamigahara City, [https://www.tezikarao.org/news/detail.php?id=149 Kakamigahara City Kinpei Five Shrines Tour], Gifu Prefectural Shrine Agency Kakamigahara City Branch. === Summary ==={{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041}} ({{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jokan}}6), founded by {{ill|lt=Emperor Seiwa|Q349243|WD=Q349243}}. In the past, this area was ruled by {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854}} such as {{ill|lt=Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899}}, and the place name ``Sohara'' is said to have come from the Soga clan's possession. It is said that after Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro passed away, the people of Sohara Manor (currently Sohara) worshiped him and held rituals there, and the shrine itself may have existed in the {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089}}. It is said that around {{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239}} ({{ill|lt=Enki|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}}5), it was located in {{ill|lt=Kakamami District|Q11412369|WD=Q11412369}} Soharasho Old Market and was called Hachiman Shrine. It is said that it was called Kasami Myojin in {{ill|lt=908|Q30463|WD=Q30463}} (Engi 8). Furthermore, according to the middle volume of "{{ill|lt=Prefectural Gosha Meiji Shrine Journals|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|lt=Fugata Gosha Meiji Shrine Journals}}" published {{ill|lt=1912|Q2077|WD=Q2077}} (Meiji 45), the {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}} and the year of its founding are unknown{{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Gifu Prefecture |journal={{ill|lt=Prefecture Gosha Meiji Shrine Magazine|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|lt=Prefectural Gosha Meiji Shrine Magazine}} |volume=Volume 2 |publisher=Meiji Shrine Magazine |date=1912-01-15 |pages=36-37 |doi=10.11501/1088278}}.In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}, there is "Kasami Shrine" in the seven temples of Kagami District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|lt=Minokuni}} Kaminashita |journal={{ill|lt=Kokushi Taikei|Q10926780|WD=Q10926780|lt=Kokushi Taikei}} |volume=13 |publisher={{ill|lt=Keizai Zasshisha|Q11606486|WD=Q11606486}} |date=1900-11-15 |pages=346 |doi=10.11501/991103}}, "Minokuni Shinmecho" lists "{{ill|lt=Senior Fourth Rank|Q11123338|WD=Q11123338|lt=Senior Fourth Rank Lower}}Kasamita Myojin" among the 23 shrines in Kagami-gunza{{Cite journal |Japanese book |editor={{ill|lt=Yiyoshi Saeki|Q11382847|WD=Q11382847}} etc. |title=Enryakuji Hon Mino Province Shinmecho |journal=Jingi Zensho |volume=Volume 4 |publisher={{ill|lt=Koten Kokyujo|Q11580997|WD=Q11580997}} |date=1908-05-17 |pages=624 |doi=10.11501/991328}}{{Cite journal |Japanese book |editor=Kakamigahara City Board of Education |title=Section 2 Shrines as seen in the Engishiki Shinmyocho |journal=Kakamigahara City History |volume=General history Nature, primitive, ancient, medieval |publisher=Kakamigahara City |date=1986-12-20 |pages=626-630 |doi=10.11501/9571788}}. In addition, in the "Kakamigahara City History", "{{ill|lt=Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}" August 15, 864 {{ill||lt=Kimi|UNKNOWN}}Article ``{{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}{{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|lt=Junior Fifth Rank Lower}}Kasami Kamishōku TwoJunior Fifth Rank UpperwoOne'', but at the time of {{Efn|{{ill|lt=quote|Q206287|WD=Q206287}}, {{ill|lt=Chinese numerals|Q683566|WD=Q683566}} was changed to {{ill|lt=Arabic numerals|Q29961325|WD=Q29961325}}. }}, the description varies depending on the manuscript. In the ``Nihon Sandai Jiroku'' published in {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}}3), which is based on ``Mino Province'', {{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Third Dynasty Actual Record Volume 9 Emperor Seiwa (August 6th year of Teikō) |journal=Japan's Third Dynasty Actual Record |publisher=Keizai Zasshisha |date=1914-02-28 |pages=184 |quote=15th Kimimi. Mino Province Junior Fifth Rank Shimonagata no Kami. Mengami Kanda God. Kasamikami. (omitted) Parallel class2Junior Fifth Rank Upper1 {{j alink|1944|Q5268}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 19th year) "Japan Three Generations Jitsuroku" says "{{ill|lt=Mimasakakuni|Q936036|WD=Q936036}}"{{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Third Dynasty Actual Record Volume 9 Emperor Seiwa (August 1987) |journal=Japan's Three Dynasty Actual Record |publisher=Daiyashu Publishing Founding Office |date=1944-06-28 |pages=140 |quote=15th Kimi. Mimasakakuni Jugoi Shimonagata no Kami. Mengami Kanda God. Former shrine deity. (omitted) Parallel class 2Junior Fifth Rank Upper 1 |doi=10.11501/3456452}}. }}.In the precincts there are {{ill|lt=Amida-do|Q11657414|WD=Q11657414}} and {{ill|lt=Yakushi-do|Q11622438|WD=Q11622438}}, and there are remnants of {{ill|lt=Shinto-Buddhist syncretism|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}}. Nemotoji once existed as {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}, but in the early Meiji period, it became {{ill|lt=Abandoned Temple|Q11486287|WD=Q11486287}} in {{ill|lt=Haibutsu Kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298}}. The main shrine (built in {{ill|lt=1687|Q7668|WD=Q7668}}), the shrine (built in {{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075}}), and the worship hall (built in {{ill|lt=1746|Q7589|WD=Q7589}}) are |2008|Q1069941}} |archiveurl=https://warp.da.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2611608/www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami1.html |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{Cultural Heritage Online|186933|Kasami Shrine Worship Hall}}``Shikiuchi Kinpeisha Kasami Shrine Bookmark''.It has a deep connection with the Soga clan, and in the surrounding area there are the tomb of Soga Ishikawamaro ({{ill|lt=legendary Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro's tomb|Q11381424|WD=Q11381424}}) and the ruins of Yamada-dera Temple ({{ill|lt=Yamada-dera Pagoda Heart Foundation|Q11470319|WD=Q11470319}}). {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}50th year) On September 16th, it was designated as Kinpeisha by Gifu Prefecture Shinto President Designation No. 76{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=780 |title=Kasami Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefectural Shrine Agency |accessdate=2025-05-09}}.{{ill|lt=Sando|Q667783|WD=Q667783}} corresponds to a part of {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City#City Road Common Name|Q641491|WD=Q641491|lt=Sohara Chuo-dori}}, and {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}} is located along the approach to Sohara Furuichiba-cho 2-chome. In the past, there were two torii gates and three torii gates on the approach to the shrine, and the latter gate was connected to Sowara Chuo Dori and {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City#Common names of roads in the city|Q641491|WD=Q641491|lt=Ogase Kaido}}. The intersection, Sannotorii, is said to have existed at the intersection of Sowara Chuo-dori Street and {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City#Common name for roads in the city|Q641491|WD=Q641491|lt=Yamada-dera Street}}{{Cite book |Japanese book |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/008/305/03_kasami240815.pdf |title=Kakamigahara City District Landscape Plan Landscape Formation Standards Guidelines Key Scenic District 3 Kasami Shrine District |format=PDF |publisher=Kakamigahara City |page=1 |accessdate=2025-02-26}}.Kasami Shrine once had Ichinomiya, Ninomiya, and Sannomiya, and Ichinomiya was the Shinmei Shrine in Furuichiba Village, Kagami District (now Sohara Furuichiba Town). (Kakamigahara City, Sohara Furuichiba Town)|Q134987025|Shinmei Shrine}}, Ninomiya is {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine} in Ibuki Village, Kakamiga District (currently {{ill|lt=Sohara Ibuki Town|Q131900799|WD=Q131900799}}) (Sohara Ibuki-cho, Kakamigahara City)|Q134928112|Yasaka Shrine}}, Sannomiya is Kumano Shrine {{Efn|{{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057}} (Meiji 42), {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine (Sohara Noguchi Town, Kakamigahara City)|Q134926762|WD=Q134926762|lt=Shinmei Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Sohara Noguchi Town|Q131870111|WD=Q131870111}}). }}From the local commentary board ``Kasami Shrine "Nino Torii" base stone''. Based on the Kakamigahara City {{ill|lt=Landscape Ordinance|Q11514689|WD=Q11514689|lt=Urban Landscape Ordinance}}, on August 1, 2008|Q2004, Kakamigahara City Notification No. 80 designated approximately 37.29{{ill|lt=ha|Q35852|WD=Q35852}} surrounding the shrine as a key scenic area.{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/shisei/shisaku/1008207/1008297/1008302/1008305.html |title=Important scenic areas (determined) Districts with historical resources and scenery |publisher=Kakamigahara City |date=2024-08-16 |accessdate=2025-05-09}}.During the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, Kasami Shrine was called {{ill|lt=Hachimangu|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939}}, and Mt. Kasami on the mountain behind the shrine was also called Mt. Hachiman, and {{ill|lt=Hachimanyama Castle (Mino Province)|Q38276751|WD=Q38276751|lt=Hachimanyama Castle}} === Enshrined God === * Kasami Okami ** According to one theory, ``Kasamiyama'' itself, located to the west-northwest of Kasami Shrine, was once an object of worship. It is assumed that Kasami Okami considered Mt. Kasami as a god. * {{ill|lt=Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} === Precinct Company === * Akiba Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Katsuchijin}}) * Okuwa Shrine ({{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Okami}}) * Kasami Inari Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kuraina Tama no Mikoto}}) * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}) === Others === * {{ill|lt=Koshin Tower|Q11486001|WD=Q11486001}} ** Built in {{ill|lt=1980|Q2439|WD=Q2439}} (1980) using one of the cornerstones of the former second torii gate of Kasami Shrine. * {{ill|lt=Loyalty Monument|Q11274518|WD=Q11274518}} ({{ill|lt=Sino-Japanese War|Q178687|WD=Q178687|lt=Conquering and Qing Monument}}, {{ill|lt=Russo-Japanese War|Q159950|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}} Loyalty Monument, Martyred Soldiers Monument) ** It was originally installed in a different location, but was moved to the shrine grounds. === Main rituals === Based on "Shikiuchi Kinpeisha Kasami Shrine Bookmark". * January 1st - {{ill|lt=Saidan Festival|Q11546824|WD=Q11546824}}, Good Luck Prayer Festival, Traffic Safety Festival * February Setsubun - {{ill|lt=Setsubun|Q746798|WD=Q746798}} Festival * February 14th of the lunar calendar - Akiba Festival * February 20th - {{ill|lt=prayer festival|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}}・Okuwa Festival * March 15th - Sumiyoshi Festival * First Saturday of April - Okunomiya Festival/Suijin Festival * Last Sunday of July - {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910}} Shinto ritual * Second Sunday before October 15th - {{ill|lt=Reitaisai|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|lt=Reitaisai}} ** It used to be held on October 14th and 15th every year. A flower mikoshi is carried out. Previously, the Kenka Mikoshi was dedicated, but it was temporarily suspended due to an accident, but the "Rokken Mikoshi Association" centered around {{ill|lt=Sohara Rokkencho|Q131900816|WD=Q131900816}} has revived the Kenka Mikoshi as a mock performance.{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://rokken-mikoshi.jimdosite.com/ |title=Rokuken Mikoshi Association |accessdate=2025-05-09}}{{Cite news |Japanese book |url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/791540 |title=Vertically ``Fighting Mikoshi'' Regular festival at Kakamigahara Kasami Shrine |newspaper={{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|WD=Q904678}} |date=2023-10-19}}.* November 15th - {{ill|lt=Shichigosan|Q1072387|WD=Q1072387}} Festival * November 23rd - New Grain Thanksgiving * December 31st - {{ill|lt=New Year's Eve|Q7245702|WD=Q7245702}} Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Monthly Festival === Cultural assets === * Wooden guardian dog ** A wooden {{ill|lt=guardian dog|Q20155846|WD=Q20155846}} approximately 115cm tall. It is thought to have been produced in the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} period. City-designated cultural property ({{ill|lt=1974|Q2478|WD=Q2478}} (June 26, 1974)) {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-designated cultural property |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005217.html |title=Is there actually only one “guardian dog”? Elegant wooden sculpture |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}. * Kasami Shrine Lion Head** A wooden lion head donated by Noguchi Village, Kagami District in {{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}}3 ({{ill|lt=1675|Q7624|WD=Q7624}}). During the annual shrine festival procession in October, a lion head leads the mikoshi procession. City-designated cultural property ({{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|WD=Q2474}} (March 23, 1974)) {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005225.html |title=A regional treasure where the legend of "Oshishi-sama" is passed down |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}. === Location === * {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491}}{{ill|lt=Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109|WD=Q131870109}} 5-chome 1 === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=Gifu Shared Car|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus}} Get off at Ozaki Danchi Line "Sohara" {{ill|lt=Bus Stop|Q953806|WD=Q953806|lt=Bus Stop}} and walk for about 10 minutes. ** {{ill|lt=JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal|Q11226229|WD=Q11226229}} Platform 13 "Suwayama Danchi" "{{ill|lt=Gifu Prefectural Kakamigahara High School|Q11471490|WD=Q11471490|lt=Kakamigahara High School}}" "{{ill|lt=Techno Plaza (Gifu Prefecture)|Q11319203|WD=Q11319203|lt=Techno Plaza}}" and "{{ill|lt=Mikakino Station|Q6845663|WD=Q6845663}}". ** {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Bus Stop|Q11415771|WD=Q11415771}} Take the bus bound for ``Suwayama Danchi,'' ``Kakamigahara High School,'' ``Techno Plaza,'' and ``Mikakino Station'' at bus stop 6. * {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316|WD=Q11412316}} ** Get off at Sohara Line "Sohara Furuichibacho" bus stop and walk for about 8 minutes. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Terikio Kasami Shrine oratory (February 2011) | Address={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491}}{{ill|lt=Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109|WD=Q131870109}} 5-chome 1 | location= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = Kasami Okami
{{ill|lt=Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}
{{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} | Founded={{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}}6 years ({{ill|lt=864 years|Q25041|WD=Q25041}}) | Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 14th|Q2922|WD=Q2922}}・{{ill|lt=October 15th|Q2919|WD=Q2919|lt=15th}} |Refer to Shinto rituals={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=#Main rituals|UNKNOWN}}| Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kasami Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kasami Shrine'' (Kasami Shrine) is {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491}}{{ill|lt=Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109|WD=Q131870109}}. Located on the left bank of {{ill|lt=Shinsakaigawa|Q11501642|WD=Q11501642}}, east-southeast of Mt. Kasami.{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikiuchisha}}. Originally 10 villages of Sohara ({{ill|lt=Ibishima Village|Q18990514|WD=Q18990514|lt=Ibuki, Asuka, Shimazaki}}, {{ill|lt=Wago Village (Inaba District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q23940976|WD=Q23940976|lt=Noguchi, Kumada, Higashijima, Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Omiya Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q18748127|WD=Q18748127|lt=Oshima/Miyashiro}}・{{ill|lt=Furuichi Market Village (Kakamami District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q22125133|WD=Q22125133|lt=Furuichi Market}}) is {{ill|lt=Soja|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129}}, but it is currently considered to be the Sojajin of {{ill|lt=Sohara Town|Q11625179|WD=Q11625179|lt=Sohara District}} and {{ill|lt=Udojin|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097}}. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is a Gosha. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} five shrines in Kakamigahara City, [https://www.tezikarao.org/news/detail.php?id=149 Kakamigahara City Kinpei Five Shrines Tour], Gifu Prefectural Shrine Agency Kakamigahara City Branch. === Summary ==={{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041}} ({{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jokan}}6), founded by {{ill|lt=Emperor Seiwa|Q349243|WD=Q349243}}. In the past, this area was ruled by {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854}} such as {{ill|lt=Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899}}, and the place name ``Sohara'' is said to have come from the Soga clan's possession. It is said that after Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro passed away, the people of Sohara Manor (currently Sohara) worshiped him and held rituals there, and the shrine itself may have existed in the {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089}}. It is said that around {{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239}} ({{ill|lt=Enki|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}}5), it was located in {{ill|lt=Kakamami District|Q11412369|WD=Q11412369}} Soharasho Old Market and was called Hachiman Shrine. It is said that it was called Kasami Myojin in {{ill|lt=908|Q30463|WD=Q30463}} (Engi 8). Furthermore, according to the middle volume of "{{ill|lt=Prefectural Gosha Meiji Shrine Journals|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|lt=Fugata Gosha Meiji Shrine Journals}}" published {{ill|lt=1912|Q2077|WD=Q2077}} (Meiji 45), the {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}} and the year of its founding are unknown{{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Gifu Prefecture |journal={{ill|lt=Prefecture Gosha Meiji Shrine Magazine|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|lt=Prefectural Gosha Meiji Shrine Magazine}} |volume=Volume 2 |publisher=Meiji Shrine Magazine |date=1912-01-15 |pages=36-37 |doi=10.11501/1088278}}.In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}, there is "Kasami Shrine" in the seven temples of Kagami District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|lt=Minokuni}} Kaminashita |journal={{ill|lt=Kokushi Taikei|Q10926780|WD=Q10926780|lt=Kokushi Taikei}} |volume=13 |publisher={{ill|lt=Keizai Zasshisha|Q11606486|WD=Q11606486}} |date=1900-11-15 |pages=346 |doi=10.11501/991103}}, "Minokuni Shinmecho" lists "{{ill|lt=Senior Fourth Rank|Q11123338|WD=Q11123338|lt=Senior Fourth Rank Lower}}Kasamita Myojin" among the 23 shrines in Kagami-gunza{{Cite journal |Japanese book |editor={{ill|lt=Yiyoshi Saeki|Q11382847|WD=Q11382847}} etc. |title=Enryakuji Hon Mino Province Shinmecho |journal=Jingi Zensho |volume=Volume 4 |publisher={{ill|lt=Koten Kokyujo|Q11580997|WD=Q11580997}} |date=1908-05-17 |pages=624 |doi=10.11501/991328}}{{Cite journal |Japanese book |editor=Kakamigahara City Board of Education |title=Section 2 Shrines as seen in the Engishiki Shinmyocho |journal=Kakamigahara City History |volume=General history Nature, primitive, ancient, medieval |publisher=Kakamigahara City |date=1986-12-20 |pages=626-630 |doi=10.11501/9571788}}. In addition, in the "Kakamigahara City History", "{{ill|lt=Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}" August 15, 864 {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kimi|UNKNOWN}}Article ``{{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}{{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|lt=Junior Fifth Rank Lower}}Kasami Kamishōku TwoJunior Fifth Rank UpperwoOne'', but at the time of {{Efn|{{ill|lt=quote|Q206287|WD=Q206287}}, {{ill|lt=Chinese numerals|Q683566|WD=Q683566}} was changed to {{ill|lt=Arabic numerals|Q29961325|WD=Q29961325}}. }}, the description varies depending on the manuscript. In the ``Nihon Sandai Jiroku'' published in {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}}3), which is based on ``Mino Province'', {{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Third Dynasty Actual Record Volume 9 Emperor Seiwa (August 6th year of Teikō) |journal=Japan's Third Dynasty Actual Record |publisher=Keizai Zasshisha |date=1914-02-28 |pages=184 |quote=15th Kimimi. Mino Province Junior Fifth Rank Shimonagata no Kami. Mengami Kanda God. Kasamikami. (omitted) Parallel class2Junior Fifth Rank Upper1 {{j alink|1944|Q5268}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 19th year) "Japan Three Generations Jitsuroku" says "{{ill|lt=Mimasakakuni|Q936036|WD=Q936036}}"{{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Third Dynasty Actual Record Volume 9 Emperor Seiwa (August 1987) |journal=Japan's Three Dynasty Actual Record |publisher=Daiyashu Publishing Founding Office |date=1944-06-28 |pages=140 |quote=15th Kimi. Mimasakakuni Jugoi Shimonagata no Kami. Mengami Kanda God. Former shrine deity. (omitted) Parallel class 2Junior Fifth Rank Upper 1 |doi=10.11501/3456452}}. }}.In the precincts there are {{ill|lt=Amida-do|Q11657414|WD=Q11657414}} and {{ill|lt=Yakushi-do|Q11622438|WD=Q11622438}}, and there are remnants of {{ill|lt=Shinto-Buddhist syncretism|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}}. Nemotoji once existed as {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}, but in the early Meiji period, it became {{ill|lt=Abandoned Temple|Q11486287|WD=Q11486287}} in {{ill|lt=Haibutsu Kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298}}. The main shrine (built in {{ill|lt=1687|Q7668|WD=Q7668}}), the shrine (built in {{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075}}), and the worship hall (built in {{ill|lt=1746|Q7589|WD=Q7589}}) are |2008|Q1069941}} |archivedate=2011-08-06}}{{Cultural Heritage Online|186933|Kasami Shrine Worship Hall}}``Shikiuchi Kinpeisha Kasami Shrine Bookmark''.It has a deep connection with the Soga clan, and in the surrounding area there are the tomb of Soga Ishikawamaro ({{ill|lt=legendary Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro's tomb|Q11381424|WD=Q11381424}}) and the ruins of Yamada-dera Temple ({{ill|lt=Yamada-dera Pagoda Heart Foundation|Q11470319|WD=Q11470319}}). {{ill|lt=1975|Q2479|WD=Q2479}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}50th year) On September 16th, it was designated as Kinpeisha by Gifu Prefecture Shinto President Designation No. 76{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=780 |title=Kasami Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefectural Shrine Agency |accessdate=2025-05-09}}.{{ill|lt=Sando|Q667783|WD=Q667783}} corresponds to a part of {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City#City Road Common Name|Q641491|WD=Q641491|lt=Sohara Chuo-dori}}, and {{ill|lt=Otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117}} is located along the approach to Sohara Furuichiba-cho 2-chome. In the past, there were two torii gates and three torii gates on the approach to the shrine, and the latter gate was connected to Sowara Chuo Dori and {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City#Common names of roads in the city|Q641491|WD=Q641491|lt=Ogase Kaido}}. The intersection, Sannotorii, is said to have existed at the intersection of Sowara Chuo-dori Street and {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City#Common name for roads in the city|Q641491|WD=Q641491|lt=Yamada-dera Street}}{{Cite book |Japanese book |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/008/305/03_kasami240815.pdf |title=Kakamigahara City District Landscape Plan Landscape Formation Standards Guidelines Key Scenic District 3 Kasami Shrine District |format=PDF |publisher=Kakamigahara City |page=1 |accessdate=2025-02-26}}.Kasami Shrine once had Ichinomiya, Ninomiya, and Sannomiya, and Ichinomiya was the Shinmei Shrine in Furuichiba Village, Kagami District (now Sohara Furuichiba Town). (Kakamigahara City, Sohara Furuichiba Town)|Q134987025|Shinmei Shrine}}, Ninomiya is {{jalink|Yasaka Shrine} in Ibuki Village, Kakamiga District (currently {{ill|lt=Sohara Ibuki Town|Q131900799|WD=Q131900799}}) (Sohara Ibuki-cho, Kakamigahara City)|Q134928112|Yasaka Shrine}}, Sannomiya is Kumano Shrine {{Efn|{{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057}} (Meiji 42), {{ill|lt=Shinmei Shrine (Sohara Noguchi Town, Kakamigahara City)|Q134926762|WD=Q134926762|lt=Shinmei Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Sohara Noguchi Town|Q131870111|WD=Q131870111}}). }}From the local commentary board ``Kasami Shrine "Nino Torii" base stone''. Based on the Kakamigahara City {{ill|lt=Landscape Ordinance|Q11514689|WD=Q11514689|lt=Urban Landscape Ordinance}}, on August 1, 2008|Q2004, Kakamigahara City Notification No. 80 designated approximately 37.29{{ill|lt=ha|Q35852|WD=Q35852}} surrounding the shrine as a key scenic area.{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/shisei/shisaku/1008207/1008297/1008302/1008305.html |title=Important scenic areas (determined) Districts with historical resources and scenery |publisher=Kakamigahara City |date=2024-08-16 |accessdate=2025-05-09}}.During the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, Kasami Shrine was called {{ill|lt=Hachimangu|Q11390939|WD=Q11390939}}, and Mt. Kasami on the mountain behind the shrine was also called Mt. Hachiman, and {{ill|lt=Hachimanyama Castle (Mino Province)|Q38276751|WD=Q38276751|lt=Hachimanyama Castle}} === Enshrined God === * Kasami Okami ** According to one theory, ``Kasamiyama'' itself, located to the west-northwest of Kasami Shrine, was once an object of worship. It is assumed that Kasami Okami considered Mt. Kasami as a god. * {{ill|lt=Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} === Precinct Company === * Akiba Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Katsuchijin}}) * Okuwa Shrine ({{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Okami}}) * Kasami Inari Shrine ({{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kuraina Tama no Mikoto}}) * Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602}}) === Others === * {{ill|lt=Koshin Tower|Q11486001|WD=Q11486001}} ** Built in {{ill|lt=1980|Q2439|WD=Q2439}} (1980) using one of the cornerstones of the former second torii gate of Kasami Shrine. * {{ill|lt=Loyalty Monument|Q11274518|WD=Q11274518}} ({{ill|lt=Sino-Japanese War|Q178687|WD=Q178687|lt=Conquering and Qing Monument}}, {{ill|lt=Russo-Japanese War|Q159950|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}} Loyalty Monument, Martyred Soldiers Monument) ** It was originally installed in a different location, but was moved to the shrine grounds. === Main rituals === Based on "Shikiuchi Kinpeisha Kasami Shrine Bookmark". * January 1st - {{ill|lt=Saidan Festival|Q11546824|WD=Q11546824}}, Good Luck Prayer Festival, Traffic Safety Festival * February Setsubun - {{ill|lt=Setsubun|Q746798|WD=Q746798}} Festival * February 14th of the lunar calendar - Akiba Festival * February 20th - {{ill|lt=prayer festival|Q118906710|WD=Q118906710}}・Okuwa Festival * March 15th - Sumiyoshi Festival * First Saturday of April - Okunomiya Festival/Suijin Festival * Last Sunday of July - {{ill|lt=Great Purification|Q11261910|WD=Q11261910}} Shinto ritual * Second Sunday before October 15th - {{ill|lt=Reitaisai|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|lt=Reitaisai}} ** It used to be held on October 14th and 15th every year. A flower mikoshi is carried out. Previously, the Kenka Mikoshi was dedicated, but it was temporarily suspended due to an accident, but the "Rokken Mikoshi Association" centered around {{ill|lt=Sohara Rokkencho|Q131900816|WD=Q131900816}} has revived the Kenka Mikoshi as a mock performance.{{Cite web |Japanese book |url=https://rokken-mikoshi.jimdosite.com/ |title=Rokuken Mikoshi Association |accessdate=2025-05-09}}{{Cite news |Japanese book |url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/791540 |title=Vertically ``Fighting Mikoshi'' Regular festival at Kakamigahara Kasami Shrine |newspaper={{ill|lt=Chunichi Shimbun|Q904678|WD=Q904678}} |date=2023-10-19}}.* November 15th - {{ill|lt=Shichigosan|Q1072387|WD=Q1072387}} Festival * November 23rd - New Grain Thanksgiving * December 31st - {{ill|lt=New Year's Eve|Q7245702|WD=Q7245702}} Festival * 1st and 15th of every month - Monthly Festival === Cultural assets === * Wooden guardian dog ** A wooden {{ill|lt=guardian dog|Q20155846|WD=Q20155846}} approximately 115cm tall. It is thought to have been produced in the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} period. City-designated cultural property ({{ill|lt=1974|Q2478|WD=Q2478}} (June 26, 1974)) {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1005178.html |title=City-designated cultural property |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005217.html |title=Is there actually only one “guardian dog”? Elegant wooden sculpture |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}. * Kasami Shrine Lion Head** A wooden lion head donated by Noguchi Village, Kagami District in {{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}}3 ({{ill|lt=1675|Q7624|WD=Q7624}}). During the annual shrine festival procession in October, a lion head leads the mikoshi procession. City-designated cultural property ({{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|WD=Q2474}} (March 23, 1974)) {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.kakamigahara.lg.jp/kankobunka/bunkazai/1011515/1005225.html |title=A regional treasure where the legend of "Oshishi-sama" is passed down |access-date=2025-03-04 |publisher=Kakamigahara City}}. === Location === * {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491}}{{ill|lt=Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109|WD=Q131870109}} 5-chome 1 === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=Gifu Shared Car|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus}} Get off at Ozaki Danchi Line "Sohara" {{ill|lt=Bus Stop|Q953806|WD=Q953806|lt=Bus Stop}} and walk for about 10 minutes. ** {{ill|lt=JR Gifu Station Bus Terminal|Q11226229|WD=Q11226229}} Platform 13 "Suwayama Danchi" "{{ill|lt=Gifu Prefectural Kakamigahara High School|Q11471490|WD=Q11471490|lt=Kakamigahara High School}}" "{{ill|lt=Techno Plaza (Gifu Prefecture)|Q11319203|WD=Q11319203|lt=Techno Plaza}}" and "{{ill|lt=Mikakino Station|Q6845663|WD=Q6845663}}". ** {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Gifu Bus Stop|Q11415771|WD=Q11415771}} Take the bus bound for ``Suwayama Danchi,'' ``Kakamigahara High School,'' ``Techno Plaza,'' and ``Mikakino Station'' at bus stop 6. * {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus|Q11412316|WD=Q11412316}} ** Get off at Sohara Line Kasami Shrine oratory (February 2011) | Address={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491}}{{ill|lt=Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109|WD=Q131870109}} 5-chome 1 | location= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = Kasami Okami
{{ill|lt=Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}
{{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} | Founded={{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}}6 years ({{ill|lt=864 years|Q25041|WD=Q25041}}) | Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 14th|Q2922|WD=Q2922}}・{{ill|lt=October 15th|Q2919|WD=Q2919|lt=15th}} |Refer to Shinto rituals={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=#Main rituals|1=UNKNOWN}}| Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kasami Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Kasami Shrine'' (Kasami Shrine) is {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Kakamigahara City|Q641491|WD=Q641491}}{{ill|lt=Sohara Old Market Town|Q131870109|WD=Q131870109}}. Located on the left bank of {{ill|lt=Shinsakaigawa|Q11501642|WD=Q11501642}}, east-southeast of Mt. Kasami.{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikiuchisha}}. Originally 10 villages of Sohara ({{ill|lt=Ibishima Village|Q18990514|WD=Q18990514|lt=Ibuki, Asuka, Shimazaki}}, {{ill|lt=Wago Village (Inaba District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q23940976|WD=Q23940976|lt=Noguchi, Kumada, Higashijima, Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Omiya Village (Gifu Prefecture)|Q18748127|WD=Q18748127|lt=Oshima/Miyashiro}}・{{ill|lt=Furuichi Market Village (Kakamami District, Gifu Prefecture)|Q22125133|WD=Q22125133|lt=Furuichi Market}}) is {{ill|lt=Soja|Q1107129|WD=Q1107129}}, but it is currently considered to be the Sojajin of {{ill|lt=Sohara Town|Q11625179|WD=Q11625179|lt=Sohara District}} and {{ill|lt=Udojin|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097}}. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is a Gosha. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Kinpeisha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} five shrines in Kakamigahara City, [https://www.tezikarao.org/news/detail.php?id=149 Kakamigahara City Kinpei Five Shrines Tour], Gifu Prefectural Shrine Agency Kakamigahara City Branch. === Summary ==={{ill|lt=864|Q25041|WD=Q25041}} ({{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jokan}}6), founded by {{ill|lt=Emperor Seiwa|Q349243|WD=Q349243}}. In the past, this area was ruled by {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854}} such as {{ill|lt=Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro|Q3488899|WD=Q3488899}}, and the place name ``Sohara'' is said to have come from the Soga clan's possession. It is said that after Sogakura Yamada Ishikawamaro passed away, the people of Sohara Manor (currently Sohara) worshiped him and held rituals there, and the shrine itself may have existed in the {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089}}. It is said that around {{ill|lt=905|Q24239|WD=Q24239}} ({{ill|lt=Enki|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325}}5), it was located in {{ill|lt=Kakamami District|Q11412369|WD=Q11412369}} Soharasho Old Market and was called Hachiman Shrine. It is said that it was called Kasami Myojin in {{ill|lt=908|Q30463|WD=Q30463}} (Engi 8). Furthermore, according to the middle volume of "{{ill|lt=Prefectural Gosha Meiji Shrine Journals|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|lt=Fugata Gosha Meiji Shrine Journals}}" published {{ill|lt=1912|Q2077|WD=Q2077}} (Meiji 45), the {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100}} and the year of its founding are unknown{{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Gifu Prefecture |journal={{ill|lt=Prefecture Gosha Meiji Shrine Magazine|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|lt=Prefectural Gosha Meiji Shrine Magazine}} |volume=Volume 2 |publisher=Meiji Shrine Magazine |date=1912-01-15 |pages=36-37 |doi=10.11501/1088278}}.In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}, there is "Kasami Shrine" in the seven temples of Kagami District in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|lt=Minokuni}} Kaminashita |journal={{ill|lt=Kokushi Taikei|Q10926780|WD=Q10926780|lt=Kokushi Taikei}} |volume=13 |publisher={{ill|lt=Keizai Zasshisha|Q11606486|WD=Q11606486}} |date=1900-11-15 |pages=346 |doi=10.11501/991103}}, "Minokuni Shinmecho" lists "{{ill|lt=Senior Fourth Rank|Q11123338|WD=Q11123338|lt=Senior Fourth Rank Lower}}Kasamita Myojin" among the 23 shrines in Kagami-gunza{{Cite journal |Japanese book |editor={{ill|lt=Yiyoshi Saeki|Q11382847|WD=Q11382847}} etc. |title=Enryakuji Hon Mino Province Shinmecho |journal=Jingi Zensho |volume=Volume 4 |publisher={{ill|lt=Koten Kokyujo|Q11580997|WD=Q11580997}} |date=1908-05-17 |pages=624 |doi=10.11501/991328}}{{Cite journal |Japanese book |editor=Kakamigahara City Board of Education |title=Section 2 Shrines as seen in the Engishiki Shinmyocho |journal=Kakamigahara City History |volume=General history Nature, primitive, ancient, medieval |publisher=Kakamigahara City |date=1986-12-20 |pages=626-630 |doi=10.11501/9571788}}. In addition, in the "Kakamigahara City History", "{{ill|lt=Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}" August 15, 864 {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kimi|1=UNKNOWN}}Article ``{{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}{{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|lt=Junior Fifth Rank Lower}}Kasami Kamishōku TwoJunior Fifth Rank UpperwoOne'', but at the time of {{Efn|{{ill|lt=quote|Q206287|WD=Q206287}}, {{ill|lt=Chinese numerals|Q683566|WD=Q683566}} was changed to {{ill|lt=Arabic numerals|Q29961325|WD=Q29961325}}. }}, the description varies depending on the manuscript. In the ``Nihon Sandai Jiroku'' published in {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|WD=Q329174}}3), which is based on ``Mino Province'', {{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Third Dynasty Actual Record Volume 9 Emperor Seiwa (August 6th year of Teikō) |journal=Japan's Third Dynasty Actual Record |publisher=Keizai Zasshisha |date=1914-02-28 |pages=184 |quote=15th Kimimi. Mino Province Junior Fifth Rank Shimonagata no Kami. Mengami Kanda God. Kasamikami. (omitted) Parallel class2Junior Fifth Rank Upper1 {{j alink|1944|Q5268}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 19th year) "Japan Three Generations Jitsuroku" says "{{ill|lt=Mimasakakuni|Q936036|WD=Q936036}}"{{Cite journal |Japanese book |title=Third Dynasty Actual Record Volume 9 Emperor Seiwa (August 1987) |journal=Japan's Three Dynasty Actual Record |publisher=Daiyashu Publishing Founding Office |date=1944-06-28 |pages=140 |quote=15th Kimi. Mimasakakuni Jugoi Shimonagata no Kami. Mengami Kanda God. Former shrine deity. (omitted) Parallel class 2Junior Fifth Rank Upper 1 |doi=10.11501/3456452}}. }}.In the precincts there are {{ill|lt=Amida-do|Q11657414|WD=Q11657414}} and {{ill|lt=Yakushi-do|Q11622438|WD=Q11622438}}, and there are remnants of {{ill|lt=Shinto-Buddhist syncretism|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}}. Nemotoji once existed as {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}, but in the early Meiji period, it became {{ill|lt=Abandoned Temple|Q11486287|WD=Q11486287}} in {{ill|lt=Haibutsu Kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298}}. The main shrine (built in {{ill|lt=1687|Q7668|WD=Q7668}}), the shrine (built in {{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075}}), and the worship hall (built in {{ill|lt=1746|Q7589|WD=Q7589}}) are |2008|Q1069941}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}20) Registered as a national {{ill|lt=registered tangible cultural property|Q11579194|WD=Q11579194}} on April 18th {{Cite web |Japanese book |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/touyuubun/kagamiharsi/kasami3.html |title=Kasami Shrine Main Hall |publisher=Gifu Prefecture
